1	# Week 6.5 — Agent sandboxing + multi-provider LLM design
2	
3	**Status:** design, pre-implementation-plan.
4	**Predecessors:** Weeks 1–2, 3, 4–6.
5	**Successors:** Week 7–9 (k8s deployment shape) consumes this contract.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Goal
10	
11	Make the agent loop pluggable and move it out of the host process, so that:
12	
13	1. Users can pick their LLM provider (Anthropic, OpenRouter, future OpenAI/Gemini) for cost savings.
14	2. Users can pick their agent framework (our own loop, `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` / pi-session, Anthropic's `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`) for capability tradeoffs.
15	3. Agent execution runs in a sandbox pod in production and a subprocess-sandbox in local dev, with **one code path** — the same IPC protocol in every mode.
16	4. Either host or sandbox can terminate unexpectedly without losing the session (any host replica can serve any sandbox; durable inbox; clean reconnect).
17	
18	## Motivation
19	
20	Current Week 4-6 shipped a host-local chat loop (`packages/core/src/chat-loop.ts`). That's the wrong topology for production — tools execute in the host process, which violates the capability-minimization invariant (I5). It also doesn't compose with pi-session or claude-agent-sdk: both are SDK-owned-loop frameworks that run as their own process with tools resolved via callbacks, not providers pluggable into our loop.
21	
22	v1 has already proven the target shape (host↔sandbox HTTP IPC, pull-based inbox, per-session workspace commits via git). We're porting the pattern and fixing v1's two known weaknesses: in-memory inbox bound to a single host replica (no failover) and no graceful cancellation path.
23	
24	## Scope: four sequenced slices
25	
26	Each slice is independently reviewable with a clean "nothing observable changes except the intended thing" acceptance. Smaller than 6.5a-as-one-blob (which would be bigger than Week 4–6 was) and targets risk where it actually lives — the topology shift dominates difficulty; providers and alternative runners are additive.
27	
28	| Slice | Ships | Acceptance |
29	|---|---|---|
30	| **6.5a — topology shift** | IPC protocol, IPC server, `sandbox:open-session`, `@ax/session-inmemory`, `@ax/chat-orchestrator`, `@ax/agent-runner-core`, `@ax/agent-native-runner`, tool refactor (host→sandbox). Still `@ax/llm-anthropic`; no streaming. | Existing Week 4–6 chat works end-to-end with the agent loop running in a subprocess sandbox; all existing subscribers fire correctly across the IPC boundary; no observable behavior change vs. Week 4–6. |
31	| **6.5b — providers + streaming** | `@ax/llm-openrouter` + streaming extensions to `llm.call` + `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber hook + native runner emits stream chunks + CLI renders incrementally. | Toggle provider via config; CLI renders responses incrementally. |
32	| **6.5c — pi-session runner** | `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` + runner-selection config. | Swap to pi-session runner via config; audit/subscribers fire identically; works with every `@ax/llm-*` plugin (no runner-side changes per provider). |
33	| **6.5d — claude-sdk runner** | `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` + `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` (host-side Anthropic-format proxy translating `POST /v1/messages` → `llm.call` IPC). | Swap to claude-sdk runner via config; claude's built-in tools + our IPC pre/post work together; LLM routes through proxy. |
34	
35	This doc specifies all four. The implementation-plan session writes the plan for 6.5a first.
36	
37	---
38	
39	## Core design decisions
40	
41	### D1. Agent loop runs in the sandbox, not the host
42	
43	The chat loop that lives in `packages/core/src/chat-loop.ts` today moves into a sandbox-side runner binary. The host orchestrates but does not execute the loop.
44	
45	Why:
46	- Tool execution inherits the sandbox's isolation boundary (I5).
47	- pi-session and claude-agent-sdk both own their internal loops. A pluggable loop on the host can't embed them cleanly.
48	- v1 runs this way; the pattern is validated.
49	
50	Cost: we lose in-process speed (every LLM call is an IPC round-trip). Acceptable — LLM latency dominates; IPC overhead is sub-millisecond over a unix socket.
51	
52	### D2. Sandbox is always the HTTP client; host never pushes
53	
54	All IPC traffic is sandbox-initiated. The host runs one HTTP listener (unix socket locally, cluster HTTP in k8s); the sandbox has no inbound port and serves nothing.
55	
56	Why:
57	- Single auth boundary (every inbound request to host is token-verified; sandbox grants no new capabilities).
58	- Sandbox is the untrusted side; fewer listening sockets = smaller attack surface.
59	- Matches v1's pattern.
60	
61	### D3. Host→sandbox delivery is pull-based via durable inbox
62	
63	When the host needs to deliver something to a running sandbox (follow-up user message, cancellation), it writes to a durable inbox keyed by `session_id`. The sandbox long-polls.
64	
65	| Preset | Inbox backing |
66	|---|---|
67	| Local / single-host | In-memory queue in `@ax/session-inmemory` |
68	| k8s (Week 7–9) | Postgres table in `@ax/session-postgres`, with `LISTEN/NOTIFY` wakeup on long-polls |
69	
70	Consequences:
71	- Any host replica can serve any sandbox's poll. No sticky sessions, no replica affinity.
72	- Host replica can crash mid-poll — sandbox's next poll hits a different replica, continues.
73	- Sandbox pod eviction detected by absence of polls beyond a heartbeat threshold; session marked terminated.
74	- No NATS, no JetStream, no second message broker. Inbox is a table with long-poll.
75	
76	Cancellation is an inbox entry of type `cancel`. Runner handles it gracefully and exits.
77	
78	### D4. `tool:post-call` is observation-only; `llm:pre-call` is the transformation point
79	
80	`claude-agent-sdk`'s `PostToolUse` hook is observation-only in practice — the `updatedMCPToolOutput` field is declared in the type but not read by `cli.js`. To keep subscriber behavior uniform across all three runners, we adopt observation-only for `tool:post-call` everywhere.
81	
82	Subscribers that need to **modify** content the model will see (canary redaction, memory recall injection, prompt restructuring) shift to `llm:pre-call`, which fires host-side right before every LLM request.
83	
84	Subscribers that just **observe** tool calls (audit, telemetry, memory extraction) stay on `tool:post-call`.
85	
86	Why this is actually better than tool-level redaction:
87	- `llm:pre-call` transformation catches secrets from every source, not just fresh tool output.
88	- Audit logs retain the raw, un-redacted tool output for forensics.
89	- Uniform across runners, tools, and execution locations (sandbox-local or host-mediated).
90	
91	### D5. Three runners, one IPC contract
92	
93	| Runner | Slice | Tool path | LLM path |
94	|---|---|---|---|
95	| `@ax/agent-native-runner` | 6.5a | Direct handlers with IPC pre/post | `llm.call` IPC directly |
96	| `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` | 6.5c | pi-session `customTools` callbacks with IPC pre/post | `registerApiProvider` (single `ax-ipc` registration) routes through `llm.call` IPC |
97	| `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` | 6.5d | Hybrid: claude-sdk built-ins + in-process MCP handlers, both wired via `canUseTool` + `hooks.PreToolUse`/`PostToolUse` | `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` points at host-side Anthropic-format proxy → `llm.call` IPC |
98	
99	All three fire the same host-side subscriber hooks via the same IPC events. Host observers (audit, canary, memory) don't know which runner is active.
100	
101	---
102	
103	## The sandbox ladder
104	
105	Three sandbox implementations, identical `sandbox:open-session` service hook contract.
106	
107	| Plugin | Isolation | When | 6.5a? |
108	|---|---|---|---|
109	| `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` | None — subprocess of host, shared FS & network | CI, fastest dev iteration, integration tests | ✅ extended |
110	| `@ax/sandbox-docker` | Docker container / Apple container — real FS + network isolation | Realistic local dev | Stretch |
111	| `@ax/sandbox-k8s` | k8s pod — full isolation, RBAC, NetworkPolicy | Production | Week 7–9 |
112	
113	All three spawn the runner binary, inject env (`AX_IPC_SOCKET` or `AX_IPC_URL`, `AX_SESSION_ID`, `AX_AUTH_TOKEN`), return a handle with `kill()`. The runner binary is identical in every mode. `@ax/sandbox-subprocess`'s total lack of isolation is fine for dev and CI; production never loads it.
114	
115	**Cleanup note:** Week 4–6's existing `sandbox:spawn` one-shot primitive (used by `@ax/tool-bash`) loses its host-side caller — bash moves into the sandbox. Delete in 6.5a. Revive only when a real host-side one-shot-subprocess consumer arrives (YAGNI).
116	
117	---
118	
119	## IPC protocol
120	
121	### Transport
122	
123	HTTP+JSON. Transport varies by sandbox plugin:
124	
125	| Sandbox plugin | Transport |
126	|---|---|
127	| `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` | Unix socket at `${AX_IPC_SOCKET}` |
128	| `@ax/sandbox-docker` | Unix socket bind-mounted into container |
129	| `@ax/sandbox-k8s` | Cluster HTTP — host exposes a Service reachable from pods |
130	
131	Same wire format, same actions. The runner reads `AX_IPC_SOCKET` (socket path) or `AX_IPC_URL` (http URL), otherwise indistinguishable.
132	
133	### Auth
134	
135	Per-session bearer token issued at `sandbox:open-session` time. Stored server-side in the session store (postgres in k8s, in-memory locally). Any host replica resolves the token against the store.
136	
137	Token lifetime = sandbox session lifetime. No per-turn rotation (v1's per-turn rotation was a workaround for its in-memory-replica problem, which we fix structurally).
138	
139	Revocation: mark session terminated in the store; subsequent IPC calls with that token return 401.
140	
141	### Sandbox → host actions (sync RPC)
142	
143	| Action | Payload | Response | Host-side effect |
144	|---|---|---|---|
145	| `llm.call` | `{ messages, tools?, model?, maxTokens?, temperature? }` | `{ assistantMessage, toolCalls[], stopReason, usage? }` | Fires `llm:pre-call` (transform), calls `llm:call` service hook, fires `llm:post-call` (observe) |
146	| `tool.pre-call` | `{ call: { id, name, input } }` | `{ verdict: 'allow' \| 'reject', modifiedCall?, reason? }` | Fires `tool:pre-call` subscriber chain |
147	| `tool.execute-host` | `{ call: { id, name, input } }` | `{ output }` | Dispatches `tool:execute:${name}` service hook (used for `executesIn: 'host'` tools) |
148	| `tool.list` | `{}` | `{ tools: ToolDescriptor[] }` | Returns the aggregated catalog |
149	| `workspace.commit-notify` | `{ parentVersion?, commitRef, message }` | `{ accepted: true, version, delta } \| { accepted: false, reason }` | Host's `@ax/workspace-git` fetches delta, fires `workspace:pre-apply` (veto-capable) → on accept: promotes + fires `workspace:applied` |
150	| `session.next-message` | `?cursor=N` query param; long-poll up to 30s | `{ type: 'user-message' \| 'cancel' \| 'timeout', payload?, cursor }` | Reads inbox, long-poll wakeup on NOTIFY |
151	
152	### Sandbox → host events (fire-and-forget)
153	
154	| Action | Payload | Host-side effect |
155	|---|---|---|
156	| `event.stream-chunk` | `{ reqId, text, kind: 'text' \| 'thinking' }` | Fires `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber (chat-ui SSE, CLI tty) |
157	| `event.tool-post-call` | `{ call, output, durationMs }` | Fires `tool:post-call` subscriber chain (observation only, per D4) |
158	| `event.turn-end` | `{ reqId, reason: 'user-message-wait' \| 'error' \| 'complete', usage? }` | Fires `chat:turn-end` subscriber |
159	| `event.chat-end` | `{ outcome: ChatOutcome }` | Fires `chat:end` subscriber |
160	
161	### Host → sandbox actions
162	
163	**None.** Host never pushes. All host→sandbox communication is via `session.next-message` responses.
164	
165	### Error model
166	
167	Every RPC response that isn't a success carries `{ error: { code, message } }`. Codes are enum-ish:
168	- `SESSION_INVALID` — token rejected, sandbox should exit
169	- `HOST_UNAVAILABLE` — retryable (replica died; sandbox reconnects)
170	- `VALIDATION` — payload schema error
171	- `HOOK_REJECTED` — subscriber chain rejected (e.g., `tool:pre-call` verdict reject)
172	- `INTERNAL` — unexpected host-side error
173	
174	Connection-level errors (socket close, HTTP error): sandbox retries with exponential backoff, capped at ~30s. Host session persists in the store; the next successful request resumes.
175	
176	### Schema location
177	
178	Every action schema lives in `@ax/ipc-protocol` — one pnpm package, pure types + Zod, no runtime. Both host-side plugins and sandbox-side runner import it. Single source of truth for the wire.
179	
180	---
181	
182	## Host-side plugin decomposition
183	
184	Per slice. Exactly-one-registrar per service hook is enforced at boot, so presets load one `llm:*` plugin at a time.
185	
186	### 6.5a
187	
188	| Package | Role |
189	|---|---|
190	| `@ax/ipc-protocol` | Zod schemas for every IPC action and event. Types-only package. |
191	| `@ax/ipc-server` | HTTP listener (unix socket or cluster HTTP). Auth middleware. Dispatches actions to the bus. |
192	| `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` *(extended)* | Registers `sandbox:open-session` (new) and continues to register `sandbox:spawn` until cleanup deletes it. Spawns runner as child process, mints auth token, creates unix socket, returns handle. |
193	| `@ax/session-inmemory` | Registers `session:create`, `session:resolve-token`, `session:queue-work`, `session:claim-work`, `session:terminate`. In-memory for 6.5a; Week 7–9 swaps to `@ax/session-postgres` against identical contract. |
194	| `@ax/chat-orchestrator` | Registers `chat:run`. Calls `sandbox:open-session`, injects initial user message into inbox, receives IPC events, returns outcome. Replaces the current `chat-loop.ts`. |
195	| `@ax/tool-dispatcher` *(extended)* | Registers `tool:register` (called by other plugins' `init()`), owns the catalog, registers `tool:list`, routes `tool:execute:${name}` service hooks. |
196	| `@ax/tool-bash` *(refocused)* | Declares descriptor `{ name: 'bash', executesIn: 'sandbox', ... }` via `tool:register`. No host-side execution impl — sandbox runner has it. |
197	| `@ax/tool-file-io` *(refocused)* | Same pattern — declares `read_file`, `write_file` descriptors with `executesIn: 'sandbox'`. |
198	| `@ax/llm-anthropic` *(unchanged)* | Registers `llm:call`. |
199	
200	### 6.5b (additions)
201	
202	| Package | Role |
203	|---|---|
204	| `@ax/llm-openrouter` *(new)* | Registers `llm:call`. OpenAI-compatible wire, routed to OpenRouter endpoint. |
205	| `@ax/ipc-protocol` *(extended)* | Adds streaming variant of `llm.call` and `event.stream-chunk` payload schema. |
206	| `@ax/chat-orchestrator` / `@ax/ipc-server` *(extended)* | Fire `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber hook on receipt of `event.stream-chunk` from sandbox. |
207	
208	### 6.5c (no host-side additions)
209	
210	Runner change only. Host-side plugins unchanged.
211	
212	### 6.5d (additions)
213	
214	| Package | Role |
215	|---|---|
216	| `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` *(new)* | HTTP endpoint serving `POST /v1/messages` in Anthropic wire format. Translates to `llm.call` IPC action, fires the usual `llm:pre-call` / `llm:post-call` subscribers. Used by claude-sdk runner via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`. |
217	
218	## Sandbox-side plugin decomposition
219	
220	Sandbox-side code lives in the same pnpm monorepo, different build target. `@ax/ipc-protocol` is the single source of truth for the wire, imported by both host-side plugins and sandbox-side runners.
221	
222	### 6.5a
223	
224	| Package | Role |
225	|---|---|
226	| `@ax/ipc-protocol` | Shared with host-side. |
227	| `@ax/agent-runner-core` | IPC client (HTTP over socket/http). Long-poll loop for inbox. Local tool dispatcher (decides `executesIn: 'sandbox'` local exec vs `executesIn: 'host'` IPC forward). Delta-aware commit helper (for 6.5a: stub; Week 7–9: full). |
228	| `@ax/agent-native-runner` | Port of current `chat-loop.ts` behavior. Calls `llm.call` IPC in a loop, dispatches tools via runner-core's dispatcher. Entry point binary. |
229	| `@ax/tool-bash-impl` | Sandbox-local implementation of bash. Runs commands via `child_process.spawn`. Registered with runner-core's local dispatcher. |
230	| `@ax/tool-file-io-impl` | Sandbox-local read_file, write_file. |
231	
232	### 6.5b (additions)
233	
234	| Package | Role |
235	|---|---|
236	| `@ax/agent-runner-core` *(extended)* | IPC client supports streaming `llm.call`; native runner emits `event.stream-chunk` as LLM chunks arrive. |
237	| `@ax/agent-native-runner` *(extended)* | Wires streaming through to IPC events. |
238	
239	### 6.5c (addition)
240	
241	| Package | Role |
242	|---|---|
243	| `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` | Embeds `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`. Registers one custom pi-ai api (`ax-ipc`) that routes all LLM traffic through `llm.call` IPC with the configured model identifier passed verbatim. Shims `customTools` callbacks through the runner-core dispatcher. Works with every `@ax/llm-*` host plugin. |
244	
245	### 6.5d (addition)
246	
247	| Package | Role |
248	|---|---|
249	| `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` | Embeds `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. Wires `canUseTool` + `hooks.PreToolUse` + `hooks.PostToolUse` to IPC events, registers host-mediated tools via `createSdkMcpServer`, sets `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to the host's Anthropic-format proxy endpoint. |
250	
251	Each runner is its own binary selected at sandbox-image-build or config time.
252	
253	---
254	
255	## Runner comparison
256	
257	### `@ax/agent-native-runner` (6.5a; streaming added in 6.5b)
258	
259	Simplest. A faithful port of current `chat-loop.ts` logic into the sandbox.
260	
261	```
262	while (!done) {
263	  for (let msg = await ipcCall('session.next-message', {cursor}); msg.type !== 'cancel';) {
264	    history.push({ role: 'user', content: msg.payload })
265	    loop:
266	    while (true) {
267	      const response = await ipcCall('llm.call', { messages: history, tools: catalog })
268	      history.push(response.assistantMessage)
269	      fireStreamChunk(response)  // if streaming
270	      if (response.toolCalls.length === 0) break loop
271	      for (const call of response.toolCalls) {
272	        const pre = await ipcCall('tool.pre-call', { call })
273	        if (pre.verdict === 'reject') { history.push(toolErrorMsg(pre.reason)); continue }
274	        const output = await executeToolLocally(pre.modifiedCall ?? call) ?? await ipcCall('tool.execute-host', { call })
275	        ipcEvent('event.tool-post-call', { call, output })
276	        history.push(toolResultMsg(call, output))
277	      }
278	    }
279	    ipcEvent('event.turn-end', { reason: 'user-message-wait' })
280	    cursor = msg.cursor
281	  }
282	}
283	```
284	
285	Non-streaming for 6.5a. Streaming lands in 6.5b alongside `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber work.
286	
287	### `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` (6.5c)
288	
289	Wraps `createAgentSession()` from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`. Works unchanged with every `@ax/llm-*` plugin the host supports — the runner is provider-agnostic.
290	
291	**LLM transport — single custom api, honest model name:**
292	
293	pi-ai's `registerApiProvider` is one-api-per-call, but pi-ai itself does not pre-format requests into Anthropic/OpenAI wire shape — the registered transport receives pi-ai's normalized `Context` (messages, tools, systemPrompt) plus the `Model` object. So we register ONCE with a custom api name and handle every backing provider uniformly:
294	
295	```ts
296	// Runner init (sandbox-side)
297	registerApiProvider({
298	  api: 'ax-ipc',
299	  stream: async (ctx, model, options) => {
300	    const response = await ipcCall('llm.call', {
301	      messages: normalizeMessages(ctx.messages),
302	      tools: ctx.tools,
303	      model: model.name,           // honest: configured model identifier passes through
304	      maxTokens: options.maxTokens,
305	      temperature: options.temperature,
306	    });
307	    return toPiAiResponse(response);
308	  },
309	  streamSimple: /* same */,
310	});
311	
312	// Build the Model pi-session will see
313	const model = {
314	  api: 'ax-ipc',                   // routes to our transport
315	  provider: 'ax',
316	  name: process.env.AX_MODEL,      // e.g. 'openrouter/gpt-4.1' or 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-7'
317	  baseUrl: '',                     // unused by our transport
318	};
319	```
320	
321	Host's `llm:call` service hook reads the `model` field from the IPC payload and routes to the configured `@ax/llm-*` plugin. OpenRouter, Anthropic, or any future provider — runner never changes.
322	
323	**Do not hardcode Model.name.** v1 effectively hardcoded a Claude model name regardless of the backing call; that creates a telemetry/logging mismatch. The configured identifier must flow through honestly.
324	
325	**Tools:** convert our `tool:list` catalog into pi-session's `customTools: ToolDefinition[]`. Each definition's `execute` callback does the IPC pre/post dance (per D4).
326	
327	**Event translation:** subscribe to `session.subscribe()`; map pi-session events to our IPC events (`text_delta` → `event.stream-chunk`, `tool_call` is handled by our callback, `message_end` → `event.turn-end`).
328	
329	**Message buffering:** pi-session uses `session.prompt(text)` once per turn, then `waitForIdle()`. Our runner loop pulls next-message from the inbox and calls `prompt`.
330	
331	### `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` (6.5d)
332	
333	Wraps `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`'s `query()`.
334	
335	**Built-in tool policy:** keep `Bash`, `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `TodoWrite`. Disable `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `Skill`, `Task`. The kept ones run inside the sandbox process tree (claude-sdk's `sdk.mjs` → `cli.js` → tool subprocesses are all descendants of the runner, so isolation holds).
336	
337	**Tool wiring:**
338	- `canUseTool` callback → IPC `tool.pre-call`. Works for both built-ins and MCP tools. Deny → `{behavior: 'deny', message}`; rewrite → `{behavior: 'allow', updatedInput}`.
339	- `hooks.PostToolUse` → IPC `event.tool-post-call` (fire-and-forget, observation-only per D4).
340	- `mcpServers` includes an in-process `createSdkMcpServer` registering our `executesIn: 'host'` tools (memory, web-via-host, etc., as they arrive). Each MCP handler does the IPC pre/post and returns output.
341	
342	**LLM transport:** `env: { ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL: '${AX_LLM_PROXY_URL}', ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: 'ax-proxy' }`. The host runs a small Anthropic-format proxy endpoint that translates `POST /v1/messages` into the `llm.call` IPC action. This is a new host-side component introduced in 6.5b, not 6.5a.
343	
344	**Event translation:** iterate the `AsyncGenerator<SDKMessage>`. `SDKAssistantMessage` with text content → `event.stream-chunk`. `SDKResultMessage` → `event.turn-end` + `event.chat-end` on final result.
345	
346	---
347	
348	## Tool registry
349	
350	### `ToolDescriptor` shape
351	
352	```ts
353	type ToolDescriptor = {
354	  name: string;              // e.g., 'bash', 'memory.recall'
355	  description: string;
356	  inputSchema: JsonSchema;   // JSON Schema Draft 2020-12
357	  executesIn: 'sandbox' | 'host';
358	}
359	```
360	
361	### Registration flow
362	
363	1. Plugin declares `calls: ['tool:register']` in its manifest.
364	2. In its `init()`, plugin calls `bus.call('tool:register', { ...descriptor })` for each tool it contributes.
365	3. Boot-time cycle detector ensures `@ax/tool-dispatcher` inits first (it registers `tool:register`).
366	4. For `executesIn: 'host'` tools, the same plugin also registers `tool:execute:${name}` service hook.
367	
368	### Catalog delivery
369	
370	At sandbox startup, runner calls `ipcCall('tool.list')` → receives the full catalog → builds its internal dispatcher.
371	
372	Cache-once-per-session is fine; tool set is immutable for a session's lifetime (invariant: no runtime tool registration). If future work needs dynamic tools, the runner polls or the host pushes via the inbox.
373	
374	### Why not one-plugin-per-tool
375	
376	`@ax/memory-cortex` will contribute 2–3 tools (store, recall, forget) plus subscriber hooks plus service hooks (internal). Splitting each tool into its own plugin means N manifests, N packages, N test suites for one conceptual unit. The `registers: ['tool:execute:memory.store', 'tool:execute:memory.recall', 'memory:record']` manifest list is the clean path — plugins group related capabilities.
377	
378	---
379	
380	## Workspace coordination contract
381	
382	Full implementation is Week 7–9 (`@ax/workspace-git`). The **contract** ships in 6.5a via the IPC protocol so Week 7–9 consumers land against a stable shape.
383	
384	### Flow
385	
386	1. Agent in sandbox modifies files via tools. Sandbox-side git is local.
387	2. At turn end (or per-tool-call; runner-specific), sandbox commits locally with a synthetic commit message.
388	3. Sandbox sends `workspace.commit-notify` RPC with `{ parentVersion, commitRef, message }`.
389	4. Host's `@ax/workspace-git`:
390	   - Fetches the commit via git-http-backend from the sandbox's git dir (subprocess mode: bind-mount; k8s mode: sidecar).
391	   - Builds a `WorkspaceDelta` (lazy-content-fetcher shape per ax-conventions).
392	   - Fires `workspace:pre-apply` — subscriber chain. Any subscriber can reject.
393	5. If rejected: host returns `{ accepted: false, reason }`. Sandbox `git reset --hard parentVersion`; surfaces an error to the model on next turn.
394	6. If accepted: host promotes the commit to its authoritative store, fires `workspace:applied`, returns `{ accepted: true, version, delta }`.
395	
396	### Subscribers that will hang off this contract (future slices)
397	
398	- **Skill validator** (`workspace:pre-apply`) — rejects malformed skill files.
399	- **Canary** (`workspace:pre-apply`) — rejects commits containing secrets.
400	- **Skill indexer** (`workspace:applied`) — updates search index.
401	- **Audit** (`workspace:applied`) — logs the commit.
402	
403	### Rewind semantics
404	
405	The sandbox must be able to undo a rejected commit cleanly. `@ax/agent-runner-core` provides a `commitAndNotify(parentVersion, message)` helper that:
406	1. Does `git commit`.
407	2. Calls `workspace.commit-notify`.
408	3. On rejection: `git reset --hard parentVersion`; surfaces `WorkspaceRejectedError` to the runner's tool-loop.
409	4. On accept: returns the new `version` token.
410	
411	Runners surface the rejection to the model as a tool error so the model can retry with corrections.
412	
413	### 6.5a scope
414	
415	No `@ax/workspace-git` ships. No subscribers wired. The `workspace.commit-notify` action exists in `@ax/ipc-protocol` schemas; the host's `@ax/ipc-server` returns `{ accepted: true, version: '<stub>', delta: null }` unconditionally. Agent's file edits work; no host-side diff awareness.
416	
417	6.5a acceptance test does not cover workspace. Week 7–9 is when the contract earns its keep.
418	
419	---
420	
421	## Boundary review — the five invariants
422	
423	### I1. Hook surface is transport- and storage-agnostic
424	
425	- `ToolDescriptor.executesIn: 'sandbox' | 'host'` — closed set, no backend-specific values. ✅
426	- `WorkspaceVersion` — opaque token, no git vocabulary in payloads. ✅
427	- `session.next-message` — no postgres-specific fields; `cursor` is just a monotonic int. ✅
428	- `IPC transport` — abstracted behind `@ax/ipc-protocol`; unix socket vs cluster HTTP is a per-plugin implementation detail. ✅
429	
430	### I2. No cross-plugin imports
431	
432	All cross-plugin communication via hook bus (host-side) and IPC protocol (host↔sandbox). Only `@ax/core`, `@ax/ipc-protocol`, and `@ax/agent-runner-core` are shared imports.
433	
434	The runner imports `@ax/agent-runner-core`; that's one-way. Runner does not import `@ax/tool-bash` (host-side plugin) — tool descriptors arrive over IPC; sandbox-local implementations live in separate `-impl` packages. ✅
435	
436	### I3. No half-wired plugins
437	
438	Every new 6.5a plugin is fully wired end-to-end in its shipping PR:
439	
440	- `@ax/ipc-server` → called by `@ax/chat-orchestrator` → exercised in acceptance test.
441	- `@ax/session-inmemory` → called by `@ax/chat-orchestrator` and `@ax/ipc-server`.
442	- `@ax/agent-native-runner` → exercised in acceptance test.
443	- `@ax/llm-openrouter` → exercised in acceptance test (preset toggles to it).
444	
445	No "wire this up later" packages. ✅
446	
447	### I4. One source of truth per concept
448	
449	- Tool catalog: `@ax/tool-dispatcher` only. Runners don't maintain their own registries.
450	- Session state: `@ax/session-*` only. Runner doesn't cache (reads from IPC).
451	- LLM credentials: host-side `@ax/llm-*` plugins. Sandbox never holds them. ✅
452	
453	### I5. Capabilities explicit and minimized
454	
455	- **Sandbox capabilities:** unix socket (or egress to one host URL), local filesystem scoped to workspace dir, process spawn for in-sandbox tool subprocesses. No direct DB, no cloud creds, no uncontrolled network egress.
456	- **IPC auth:** per-session bearer token, revocable, scoped to that session's IPC actions. Cannot impersonate another session.
457	- **Tool input validation:** every `tool:execute:${name}` handler validates input via Zod before execution. `tool.pre-call` subscriber chain can reject before the tool runs.
458	- **Untrusted content tagging:** LLM output and tool output are untrusted; `llm:pre-call` subscribers treat history content as untrusted.
459	
460	**Security checklist required for every slice's PR.**
461	- **6.5a:** `@ax/ipc-server` is new attack surface; sandbox primitives cross the trust boundary.
462	- **6.5b:** `@ax/llm-openrouter` is a new third-party endpoint + new dep.
463	- **6.5c:** `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` and transitive deps land inside the sandbox.
464	- **6.5d:** `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` spawns a subprocess tree inside the sandbox; `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` is new host-side HTTP surface.
465	
466	Three threat models apply throughout: sandbox escape, prompt injection, supply chain.
467	
468	---
469	
470	## Per-slice acceptance tests
471	
472	### 6.5a — topology shift
473	
474	End-to-end:
475	1. Start host process with a config that loads `@ax/sandbox-subprocess`, `@ax/session-inmemory`, `@ax/llm-anthropic`, `@ax/agent-native-runner` (via sandbox image/binary), and all tool plugins.
476	2. CLI sends a user message.
477	3. Host spawns a subprocess sandbox; runner connects over unix socket.
478	4. Runner polls inbox, receives user message, calls `llm.call` (routed to Anthropic).
479	5. LLM responds with a `bash` tool call.
480	6. Runner IPCs `tool.pre-call` → host fires subscribers → `allow`.
481	7. Runner executes bash locally in sandbox.
482	8. Runner IPCs `event.tool-post-call` → host fires `tool:post-call` (audit observes).
483	9. Runner loops back into LLM with tool result.
484	10. Final assistant message; runner fires `event.chat-end`; host returns outcome to CLI.
485	
486	Automated test variant: mock Anthropic HTTP with a canned response chain, spawn real subprocess sandbox, assert: all IPC events fired host-side, audit plugin logged, outcome message returned. **The observable behavior of this chat must match what Week 4-6's in-process loop produced** — that's the "nothing observable changes" invariant for this slice.
487	
488	### 6.5b — providers + streaming
489	
490	1. Config selects `@ax/llm-openrouter` instead of `@ax/llm-anthropic`.
491	2. CLI sends a user message.
492	3. LLM responds with streaming chunks; runner emits `event.stream-chunk` per chunk.
493	4. CLI renders text incrementally as chunks arrive.
494	5. Same tool-call flow as 6.5a completes.
495	
496	Automated test: mock OpenRouter HTTP with a streaming SSE response, assert `chat:stream-chunk` subscribers fire in order, final message matches.
497	
498	### 6.5c — pi-session runner
499	
500	1. Config sets `runner: 'pi-session'`.
501	2. Config sets `AX_MODEL` to any supported host provider's model (e.g., `openrouter/gpt-4.1`).
502	3. CLI sends a user message.
503	4. Sandbox starts pi-session-runner binary; pi-session runs its internal loop; LLM traffic routes through the `ax-ipc` custom api → `llm.call` IPC → host's `@ax/llm-openrouter` (or whichever is loaded).
504	5. Subscribers fire identically to 6.5a/6.5b (the host doesn't know pi-session is in the sandbox).
505	
506	Automated test: same as 6.5a/6.5b but with pi-session runner binary. Assert: identical host-observable event sequence.
507	
508	### 6.5d — claude-sdk runner
509	
510	1. Config sets `runner: 'claude-sdk'`.
511	2. CLI sends a user message.
512	3. Sandbox starts claude-sdk-runner binary; SDK's `query()` runs; `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` routes LLM traffic to host's Anthropic-format proxy → `llm.call` IPC.
513	4. Built-in Bash tool runs inside the sandbox (subprocess tree); `canUseTool` fires `tool.pre-call` IPC; `hooks.PostToolUse` fires `event.tool-post-call` IPC.
514	5. MCP-registered `executesIn: 'host'` tools dispatch via `tool.execute-host` IPC.
515	
516	Automated test: mock Anthropic HTTP (via the proxy), spawn real subprocess sandbox with claude-sdk-runner, assert: built-in and MCP tool calls both fire `tool:pre-call` / `tool:post-call` subscribers, outcome matches.
517	
518	---
519	
520	## Out of scope / deferred
521	
522	| Deferred to | What |
523	|---|---|
524	| **6.5b** | `@ax/llm-openrouter`, streaming `llm.call` extensions, `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber hook |
525	| **6.5c** | `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner`, runner-selection config |
526	| **6.5d** | `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`, `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` |
527	| **Week 7–9** | `@ax/sandbox-k8s`, `@ax/workspace-git` (and therefore `workspace:pre-apply` / `workspace:applied` subscribers), `@ax/session-postgres`, `@ax/eventbus-*`, multi-replica host coordination |
528	| **Week 10–12** | Canary scanner wired against `llm:pre-call`, memory-cortex tool registration, chat-ui SSE consuming `chat:stream-chunk` |
529	| **Week 13+** | Additional runner variants (other agent frameworks); hot-reload of tool catalog; parallel tool execution (current design is sequential) |
530	
531	Not in any slice (yet):
532	- Docker / apple-container sandbox plugin. Subprocess is enough for 6.5a dev; k8s is Week 7–9 for prod. Docker is nice-to-have, ships when a user actually wants it.
533	- Dynamic tool registration mid-session. Invariant for now: catalog is session-lifetime-immutable.
534	- Host→sandbox push (for admin commands, etc.). Inbox handles everything we know of; revisit if a use case appears.
535	
536	---
537	
538	## Scope decisions for the 6.5a implementation plan
539	
540	These are 6.5a-specific open questions. Each needs an explicit call before code lands. Later slices (6.5b–d) surface their own questions when their plans get written; the list below is scoped to the topology shift.
541	
542	1. **Runner binary packaging.** Single binary per runner (bundled with tool impls), or runner binary + tool impls as dlopen-able modules? Recommendation: single binary per runner. Simpler, ships in one dockerfile COPY line. Re-examine if the binary gets large enough to matter.
543	
544	2. **Auth token scheme.** Opaque random string (64 bytes base64)? JWT (signed, carries claims)? Recommendation: opaque random, verified against session store on every IPC call. JWT adds signing ceremony without clear benefit since we have the store anyway.
545	
546	3. **IPC request timeouts.** Per-action or global default? v1 uses 5min for `agent_response`, 15min safety net, per-call configurable. Recommendation: per-action defaults in `@ax/ipc-protocol` (e.g., `llm.call` = 5min, `tool.pre-call` = 10s, `session.next-message` = 30s long-poll), global override in config.
547	
548	4. **Concurrent IPC from sandbox.** Can multiple RPCs be in flight at once (e.g., streaming LLM chunks while issuing tool.pre-call for a queued call)? Recommendation: yes, HTTP supports it natively; runner-core uses an HTTP keep-alive connection pool. Ordering of fire-and-forget events is best-effort.
549	
550	5. **Cursor semantics for `session.next-message`.** Monotonic int per session? Recommendation: int; inbox entries carry their own index; cursor = last-seen-index. Sandbox advances only after processing, so crashes replay from the last-processed cursor.
551	
552	6. **Eventbus in 6.5a?** Not needed for single-host/single-replica, but `chat:stream-chunk` fan-out wants it once multi-replica arrives. Recommendation: skip for 6.5a, ship alongside `@ax/eventbus-postgres` in Week 7–9. Document the fan-out path in the design so nothing surprises us later.
553	
554	7. **How much of `chat-loop.ts` survives.** The turn-loop shape ports to the sandbox-side runner. The host-side orchestrator is thinner — it spawns the sandbox, waits for chat:end, returns outcome. Recommendation: delete `chat-loop.ts` as part of 6.5a; don't leave the dead code around.
555	
556	8. **ax.config.ts preset wiring.** `@ax/preset-local-native-anthropic`, `@ax/preset-local-native-openrouter`? Or a more flexible config-driven preset? Recommendation: keep current ax.config.ts loader; introduce named presets as sugar later when the combinatorial cost justifies them.
557	
558	---
559	
560	## Kickoff prompts for implementation-plan sessions
561	
562	Each slice gets its own implementation plan, written in a fresh session after `/clear`. The plans share `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md` as the contract doc.
563	
564	### 6.5a — topology shift
565	
566	```
567	Write an implementation plan for Week 6.5a of docs/plans/2026-04-22-plugin-architecture-design.md
568	(agent sandboxing — topology shift). Read docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md
569	first — it specifies the IPC protocol, plugin decomposition, and runner contract. Scope is
570	strictly the topology shift: @ax/ipc-protocol, @ax/ipc-server, @ax/sandbox-subprocess extensions
571	(sandbox:open-session), @ax/session-inmemory, @ax/chat-orchestrator, @ax/agent-runner-core,
572	@ax/agent-native-runner, tool refactor (host→sandbox). Keep @ax/llm-anthropic unchanged; no
573	streaming; no new providers. Acceptance: Week 4-6's chat behavior is preserved, now with the
574	loop running in a subprocess sandbox. Invoke security-checklist for @ax/ipc-server and the
575	@ax/sandbox-subprocess session-lifetime extensions. Branch off the tip of Week 4-6. The plan
576	should be executable via subagent-driven-development.
577	```
578	
579	### 6.5b — providers + streaming
580	
581	```
582	Write an implementation plan for Week 6.5b (providers + streaming). Read
583	docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md. Scope: @ax/llm-openrouter, streaming
584	extensions to llm.call IPC action, chat:stream-chunk subscriber hook, native runner streaming
585	emission, CLI incremental rendering. Acceptance: swap provider via config; CLI renders
586	responses incrementally. Invoke security-checklist for @ax/llm-openrouter (new third-party
587	endpoint). Branch off 6.5a. Executable via subagent-driven-development.
588	```
589	
590	### 6.5c — pi-session runner
591	
592	```
593	Write an implementation plan for Week 6.5c (pi-session runner). Read
594	docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md; the pi-session-runner section
595	specifies the single-ax-ipc-api registration pattern and the honest-model-identifier invariant.
596	Scope: @ax/agent-pi-session-runner, runner-selection config. Acceptance: swap to pi-session via
597	config; works with every host-side @ax/llm-* plugin without runner changes. Invoke
598	security-checklist for the new sandbox-side dependency tree (@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent
599	and its transitive deps). Branch off 6.5b. Executable via subagent-driven-development.
600	```
601	
602	### 6.5d — claude-sdk runner
603	
604	```
605	Write an implementation plan for Week 6.5d (claude-sdk runner). Read
606	docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md; the claude-sdk-runner section
607	specifies the hybrid built-in-tool policy (keep Bash/Read/Write/Edit/Glob/Grep/TodoWrite;
608	disable WebFetch/WebSearch/Skill/Task), the canUseTool/PostToolUse wiring, and the
609	Anthropic-format proxy requirement. Scope: @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner,
610	@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format. Acceptance: swap to claude-sdk via config; built-ins and MCP
611	tools both fire tool:pre-call / tool:post-call subscribers. Invoke security-checklist for
612	@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format (new HTTP surface) and for the claude-sdk dependency tree.
613	Branch off 6.5c. Executable via subagent-driven-development.
614	```
615

file.content:
# Week 6.5 — Agent sandboxing + multi-provider LLM design

**Status:** design, pre-implementation-plan.
**Predecessors:** Weeks 1–2, 3, 4–6.
**Successors:** Week 7–9 (k8s deployment shape) consumes this contract.

---

## Goal

Make the agent loop pluggable and move it out of the host process, so that:

1. Users can pick their LLM provider (Anthropic, OpenRouter, future OpenAI/Gemini) for cost savings.
2. Users can pick their agent framework (our own loop, `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` / pi-session, Anthropic's `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`) for capability tradeoffs.
3. Agent execution runs in a sandbox pod in production and a subprocess-sandbox in local dev, with **one code path** — the same IPC protocol in every mode.
4. Either host or sandbox can terminate unexpectedly without losing the session (any host replica can serve any sandbox; durable inbox; clean reconnect).

## Motivation

Current Week 4-6 shipped a host-local chat loop (`packages/core/src/chat-loop.ts`). That's the wrong topology for production — tools execute in the host process, which violates the capability-minimization invariant (I5). It also doesn't compose with pi-session or claude-agent-sdk: both are SDK-owned-loop frameworks that run as their own process with tools resolved via callbacks, not providers pluggable into our loop.

v1 has already proven the target shape (host↔sandbox HTTP IPC, pull-based inbox, per-session workspace commits via git). We're porting the pattern and fixing v1's two known weaknesses: in-memory inbox bound to a single host replica (no failover) and no graceful cancellation path.

## Scope: four sequenced slices

Each slice is independently reviewable with a clean "nothing observable changes except the intended thing" acceptance. Smaller than 6.5a-as-one-blob (which would be bigger than Week 4–6 was) and targets risk where it actually lives — the topology shift dominates difficulty; providers and alternative runners are additive.

| Slice | Ships | Acceptance |
|---|---|---|
| **6.5a — topology shift** | IPC protocol, IPC server, `sandbox:open-session`, `@ax/session-inmemory`, `@ax/chat-orchestrator`, `@ax/agent-runner-core`, `@ax/agent-native-runner`, tool refactor (host→sandbox). Still `@ax/llm-anthropic`; no streaming. | Existing Week 4–6 chat works end-to-end with the agent loop running in a subprocess sandbox; all existing subscribers fire correctly across the IPC boundary; no observable behavior change vs. Week 4–6. |
| **6.5b — providers + streaming** | `@ax/llm-openrouter` + streaming extensions to `llm.call` + `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber hook + native runner emits stream chunks + CLI renders incrementally. | Toggle provider via config; CLI renders responses incrementally. |
| **6.5c — pi-session runner** | `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` + runner-selection config. | Swap to pi-session runner via config; audit/subscribers fire identically; works with every `@ax/llm-*` plugin (no runner-side changes per provider). |
| **6.5d — claude-sdk runner** | `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` + `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` (host-side Anthropic-format proxy translating `POST /v1/messages` → `llm.call` IPC). | Swap to claude-sdk runner via config; claude's built-in tools + our IPC pre/post work together; LLM routes through proxy. |

This doc specifies all four. The implementation-plan session writes the plan for 6.5a first.

---

## Core design decisions

### D1. Agent loop runs in the sandbox, not the host

The chat loop that lives in `packages/core/src/chat-loop.ts` today moves into a sandbox-side runner binary. The host orchestrates but does not execute the loop.

Why:
- Tool execution inherits the sandbox's isolation boundary (I5).
- pi-session and claude-agent-sdk both own their internal loops. A pluggable loop on the host can't embed them cleanly.
- v1 runs this way; the pattern is validated.

Cost: we lose in-process speed (every LLM call is an IPC round-trip). Acceptable — LLM latency dominates; IPC overhead is sub-millisecond over a unix socket.

### D2. Sandbox is always the HTTP client; host never pushes

All IPC traffic is sandbox-initiated. The host runs one HTTP listener (unix socket locally, cluster HTTP in k8s); the sandbox has no inbound port and serves nothing.

Why:
- Single auth boundary (every inbound request to host is token-verified; sandbox grants no new capabilities).
- Sandbox is the untrusted side; fewer listening sockets = smaller attack surface.
- Matches v1's pattern.

### D3. Host→sandbox delivery is pull-based via durable inbox

When the host needs to deliver something to a running sandbox (follow-up user message, cancellation), it writes to a durable inbox keyed by `session_id`. The sandbox long-polls.

| Preset | Inbox backing |
|---|---|
| Local / single-host | In-memory queue in `@ax/session-inmemory` |
| k8s (Week 7–9) | Postgres table in `@ax/session-postgres`, with `LISTEN/NOTIFY` wakeup on long-polls |

Consequences:
- Any host replica can serve any sandbox's poll. No sticky sessions, no replica affinity.
- Host replica can crash mid-poll — sandbox's next poll hits a different replica, continues.
- Sandbox pod eviction detected by absence of polls beyond a heartbeat threshold; session marked terminated.
- No NATS, no JetStream, no second message broker. Inbox is a table with long-poll.

Cancellation is an inbox entry of type `cancel`. Runner handles it gracefully and exits.

### D4. `tool:post-call` is observation-only; `llm:pre-call` is the transformation point

`claude-agent-sdk`'s `PostToolUse` hook is observation-only in practice — the `updatedMCPToolOutput` field is declared in the type but not read by `cli.js`. To keep subscriber behavior uniform across all three runners, we adopt observation-only for `tool:post-call` everywhere.

Subscribers that need to **modify** content the model will see (canary redaction, memory recall injection, prompt restructuring) shift to `llm:pre-call`, which fires host-side right before every LLM request.

Subscribers that just **observe** tool calls (audit, telemetry, memory extraction) stay on `tool:post-call`.

Why this is actually better than tool-level redaction:
- `llm:pre-call` transformation catches secrets from every source, not just fresh tool output.
- Audit logs retain the raw, un-redacted tool output for forensics.
- Uniform across runners, tools, and execution locations (sandbox-local or host-mediated).

### D5. Three runners, one IPC contract

| Runner | Slice | Tool path | LLM path |
|---|---|---|---|
| `@ax/agent-native-runner` | 6.5a | Direct handlers with IPC pre/post | `llm.call` IPC directly |
| `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` | 6.5c | pi-session `customTools` callbacks with IPC pre/post | `registerApiProvider` (single `ax-ipc` registration) routes through `llm.call` IPC |
| `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` | 6.5d | Hybrid: claude-sdk built-ins + in-process MCP handlers, both wired via `canUseTool` + `hooks.PreToolUse`/`PostToolUse` | `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` points at host-side Anthropic-format proxy → `llm.call` IPC |

All three fire the same host-side subscriber hooks via the same IPC events. Host observers (audit, canary, memory) don't know which runner is active.

---

## The sandbox ladder

Three sandbox implementations, identical `sandbox:open-session` service hook contract.

| Plugin | Isolation | When | 6.5a? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` | None — subprocess of host, shared FS & network | CI, fastest dev iteration, integration tests | ✅ extended |
| `@ax/sandbox-docker` | Docker container / Apple container — real FS + network isolation | Realistic local dev | Stretch |
| `@ax/sandbox-k8s` | k8s pod — full isolation, RBAC, NetworkPolicy | Production | Week 7–9 |

All three spawn the runner binary, inject env (`AX_IPC_SOCKET` or `AX_IPC_URL`, `AX_SESSION_ID`, `AX_AUTH_TOKEN`), return a handle with `kill()`. The runner binary is identical in every mode. `@ax/sandbox-subprocess`'s total lack of isolation is fine for dev and CI; production never loads it.

**Cleanup note:** Week 4–6's existing `sandbox:spawn` one-shot primitive (used by `@ax/tool-bash`) loses its host-side caller — bash moves into the sandbox. Delete in 6.5a. Revive only when a real host-side one-shot-subprocess consumer arrives (YAGNI).

---

## IPC protocol

### Transport

HTTP+JSON. Transport varies by sandbox plugin:

| Sandbox plugin | Transport |
|---|---|
| `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` | Unix socket at `${AX_IPC_SOCKET}` |
| `@ax/sandbox-docker` | Unix socket bind-mounted into container |
| `@ax/sandbox-k8s` | Cluster HTTP — host exposes a Service reachable from pods |

Same wire format, same actions. The runner reads `AX_IPC_SOCKET` (socket path) or `AX_IPC_URL` (http URL), otherwise indistinguishable.

### Auth

Per-session bearer token issued at `sandbox:open-session` time. Stored server-side in the session store (postgres in k8s, in-memory locally). Any host replica resolves the token against the store.

Token lifetime = sandbox session lifetime. No per-turn rotation (v1's per-turn rotation was a workaround for its in-memory-replica problem, which we fix structurally).

Revocation: mark session terminated in the store; subsequent IPC calls with that token return 401.

### Sandbox → host actions (sync RPC)

| Action | Payload | Response | Host-side effect |
|---|---|---|---|
| `llm.call` | `{ messages, tools?, model?, maxTokens?, temperature? }` | `{ assistantMessage, toolCalls[], stopReason, usage? }` | Fires `llm:pre-call` (transform), calls `llm:call` service hook, fires `llm:post-call` (observe) |
| `tool.pre-call` | `{ call: { id, name, input } }` | `{ verdict: 'allow' \| 'reject', modifiedCall?, reason? }` | Fires `tool:pre-call` subscriber chain |
| `tool.execute-host` | `{ call: { id, name, input } }` | `{ output }` | Dispatches `tool:execute:${name}` service hook (used for `executesIn: 'host'` tools) |
| `tool.list` | `{}` | `{ tools: ToolDescriptor[] }` | Returns the aggregated catalog |
| `workspace.commit-notify` | `{ parentVersion?, commitRef, message }` | `{ accepted: true, version, delta } \| { accepted: false, reason }` | Host's `@ax/workspace-git` fetches delta, fires `workspace:pre-apply` (veto-capable) → on accept: promotes + fires `workspace:applied` |
| `session.next-message` | `?cursor=N` query param; long-poll up to 30s | `{ type: 'user-message' \| 'cancel' \| 'timeout', payload?, cursor }` | Reads inbox, long-poll wakeup on NOTIFY |

### Sandbox → host events (fire-and-forget)

| Action | Payload | Host-side effect |
|---|---|---|
| `event.stream-chunk` | `{ reqId, text, kind: 'text' \| 'thinking' }` | Fires `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber (chat-ui SSE, CLI tty) |
| `event.tool-post-call` | `{ call, output, durationMs }` | Fires `tool:post-call` subscriber chain (observation only, per D4) |
| `event.turn-end` | `{ reqId, reason: 'user-message-wait' \| 'error' \| 'complete', usage? }` | Fires `chat:turn-end` subscriber |
| `event.chat-end` | `{ outcome: ChatOutcome }` | Fires `chat:end` subscriber |

### Host → sandbox actions

**None.** Host never pushes. All host→sandbox communication is via `session.next-message` responses.

### Error model

Every RPC response that isn't a success carries `{ error: { code, message } }`. Codes are enum-ish:
- `SESSION_INVALID` — token rejected, sandbox should exit
- `HOST_UNAVAILABLE` — retryable (replica died; sandbox reconnects)
- `VALIDATION` — payload schema error
- `HOOK_REJECTED` — subscriber chain rejected (e.g., `tool:pre-call` verdict reject)
- `INTERNAL` — unexpected host-side error

Connection-level errors (socket close, HTTP error): sandbox retries with exponential backoff, capped at ~30s. Host session persists in the store; the next successful request resumes.

### Schema location

Every action schema lives in `@ax/ipc-protocol` — one pnpm package, pure types + Zod, no runtime. Both host-side plugins and sandbox-side runner import it. Single source of truth for the wire.

## Host-side plugin decomposition

Per slice. Exactly-one-registrar per service hook is enforced at boot, so presets load one `llm:*` plugin at a time.

### 6.5a

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `@ax/ipc-protocol` | Zod schemas for every IPC action and event. Types-only package. |
| `@ax/ipc-server` | HTTP listener (unix socket or cluster HTTP). Auth middleware. Dispatches actions to the bus. |
| `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` *(extended)* | Registers `sandbox:open-session` (new) and continues to register `sandbox:spawn` until cleanup deletes it. Spawns runner as child process, mints auth token, creates unix socket, returns handle. |
| `@ax/session-inmemory` | Registers `session:create`, `session:resolve-token`, `session:queue-work`, `session:claim-work`, `session:terminate`. In-memory for 6.5a; Week 7–9 swaps to `@ax/session-postgres` against identical contract. |
| `@ax/chat-orchestrator` | Registers `chat:run`. Calls `sandbox:open-session`, injects initial user message into inbox, receives IPC events, returns outcome. Replaces the current `chat-loop.ts`. |
| `@ax/tool-dispatcher` *(extended)* | Registers `tool:register` (called by other plugins' `init()`), owns the catalog, registers `tool:list`, routes `tool:execute:${name}` service hooks. |
| `@ax/tool-bash` *(refocused)* | Declares descriptor `{ name: 'bash', executesIn: 'sandbox', ... }` via `tool:register`. No host-side execution impl — sandbox runner has it. |
| `@ax/tool-file-io` *(refocused)* | Same pattern — declares `read_file`, `write_file` descriptors with `executesIn: 'sandbox'`. |
| `@ax/llm-anthropic` *(unchanged)* | Registers `llm:call`. |

### 6.5b (additions)

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `@ax/llm-openrouter` *(new)* | Registers `llm:call`. OpenAI-compatible wire, routed to OpenRouter endpoint. |
| `@ax/ipc-protocol` *(extended)* | Adds streaming variant of `llm.call` and `event.stream-chunk` payload schema. |
| `@ax/chat-orchestrator` / `@ax/ipc-server` *(extended)* | Fire `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber hook on receipt of `event.stream-chunk` from sandbox. |

### 6.5c (no host-side additions)

Runner change only. Host-side plugins unchanged.

### 6.5d (additions)

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` *(new)* | HTTP endpoint serving `POST /v1/messages` in Anthropic wire format. Translates to `llm.call` IPC action, fires the usual `llm:pre-call` / `llm:post-call` subscribers. Used by claude-sdk runner via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`. |

## Sandbox-side plugin decomposition

Sandbox-side code lives in the same pnpm monorepo, different build target. `@ax/ipc-protocol` is the single source of truth for the wire, imported by both host-side plugins and sandbox-side runners.

### 6.5a

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `@ax/ipc-protocol` | Shared with host-side. |
| `@ax/agent-runner-core` | IPC client (HTTP over socket/http). Long-poll loop for inbox. Local tool dispatcher (decides `executesIn: 'sandbox'` local exec vs `executesIn: 'host'` IPC forward). Delta-aware commit helper (for 6.5a: stub; Week 7–9: full). |
| `@ax/agent-native-runner` | Port of current `chat-loop.ts` behavior. Calls `llm.call` IPC in a loop, dispatches tools via runner-core's dispatcher. Entry point binary. |
| `@ax/tool-bash-impl` | Sandbox-local implementation of bash. Runs commands via `child_process.spawn`. Registered with runner-core's local dispatcher. |
| `@ax/tool-file-io-impl` | Sandbox-local read_file, write_file. |

### 6.5b (additions)

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `@ax/agent-runner-core` *(extended)* | IPC client supports streaming `llm.call`; native runner emits `event.stream-chunk` as LLM chunks arrive. |
| `@ax/agent-native-runner` *(extended)* | Wires streaming through to IPC events. |

### 6.5c (addition)

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` | Embeds `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`. Registers one custom pi-ai api (`ax-ipc`) that routes all LLM traffic through `llm.call` IPC with the configured model identifier passed verbatim. Shims `customTools` callbacks through the runner-core dispatcher. Works with every `@ax/llm-*` host plugin. |

### 6.5d (addition)

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` | Embeds `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. Wires `canUseTool` + `hooks.PreToolUse` + `hooks.PostToolUse` to IPC events, registers host-mediated tools via `createSdkMcpServer`, sets `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to the host's Anthropic-format proxy endpoint. |

Each runner is its own binary selected at sandbox-image-build or config time.

## Runner comparison

### `@ax/agent-native-runner` (6.5a; streaming added in 6.5b)

Simplest. A faithful port of current `chat-loop.ts` logic into the sandbox.

```
while (!done) {
  for (let msg = await ipcCall('session.next-message', {cursor}); msg.type !== 'cancel';) {
    history.push({ role: 'user', content: msg.payload })
    loop:
    while (true) {
      const response = await ipcCall('llm.call', { messages: history, tools: catalog })
      history.push(response.assistantMessage)
      fireStreamChunk(response)  // if streaming
      if (response.toolCalls.length === 0) break loop
      for (const call of response.toolCalls) {
        const pre = await ipcCall('tool.pre-call', { call })
        if (pre.verdict === 'reject') { history.push(toolErrorMsg(pre.reason)); continue }
        const output = await executeToolLocally(pre.modifiedCall ?? call) ?? await ipcCall('tool.execute-host', { call })
        ipcEvent('event.tool-post-call', { call, output })
        history.push(toolResultMsg(call, output))
      }
    }
    ipcEvent('event.turn-end', { reason: 'user-message-wait' })
    cursor = msg.cursor
  }
}
```

Non-streaming for 6.5a. Streaming lands in 6.5b alongside `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber work.

### `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` (6.5c)

Wraps `createAgentSession()` from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`. Works unchanged with every `@ax/llm-*` plugin the host supports — the runner is provider-agnostic.

**LLM transport — single custom api, honest model name:**

pi-ai's `registerApiProvider` is one-api-per-call, but pi-ai itself does not pre-format requests into Anthropic/OpenAI wire shape — the registered transport receives pi-ai's normalized `Context` (messages, tools, systemPrompt) plus the `Model` object. So we register ONCE with a custom api name and handle every backing provider uniformly:

```ts
// Runner init (sandbox-side)
registerApiProvider({
  api: 'ax-ipc',
  stream: async (ctx, model, options) => {
    const response = await ipcCall('llm.call', {
      messages: normalizeMessages(ctx.messages),
      tools: ctx.tools,
      model: model.name,           // honest: configured model identifier passes through
      maxTokens: options.maxTokens,
      temperature: options.temperature,
    });
    return toPiAiResponse(response);
  },
  streamSimple: /* same */,
});

// Build the Model pi-session will see
const model = {
  api: 'ax-ipc',                   // routes to our transport
  provider: 'ax',
  name: process.env.AX_MODEL,      // e.g. 'openrouter/gpt-4.1' or 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-7'
  baseUrl: '',                     // unused by our transport
};
```

Host's `llm:call` service hook reads the `model` field from the IPC payload and routes to the configured `@ax/llm-*` plugin. OpenRouter, Anthropic, or any future provider — runner never changes.

**Do not hardcode Model.name.** v1 effectively hardcoded a Claude model name regardless of the backing call; that creates a telemetry/logging mismatch. The configured identifier must flow through honestly.

**Tools:** convert our `tool:list` catalog into pi-session's `customTools: ToolDefinition[]`. Each definition's `execute` callback does the IPC pre/post dance (per D4).

**Event translation:** subscribe to `session.subscribe()`; map pi-session events to our IPC events (`text_delta` → `event.stream-chunk`, `tool_call` is handled by our callback, `message_end` → `event.turn-end`).

**Message buffering:** pi-session uses `session.prompt(text)` once per turn, then `waitForIdle()`. Our runner loop pulls next-message from the inbox and calls `prompt`.

### `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` (6.5d)

Wraps `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`'s `query()`.

**Built-in tool policy:** keep `Bash`, `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `TodoWrite`. Disable `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `Skill`, `Task`. The kept ones run inside the sandbox process tree (claude-sdk's `sdk.mjs` → `cli.js` → tool subprocesses are all descendants of the runner, so isolation holds).

**Tool wiring:**
- `canUseTool` callback → IPC `tool.pre-call`. Works for both built-ins and MCP tools. Deny → `{behavior: 'deny', message}`; rewrite → `{behavior: 'allow', updatedInput}`.
- `hooks.PostToolUse` → IPC `event.tool-post-call` (fire-and-forget, observation-only per D4).
- `mcpServers` includes an in-process `createSdkMcpServer` registering our `executesIn: 'host'` tools (memory, web-via-host, etc., as they arrive). Each MCP handler does the IPC pre/post and returns output.

**LLM transport:** `env: { ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL: '${AX_LLM_PROXY_URL}', ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: 'ax-proxy' }`. The host runs a small Anthropic-format proxy endpoint that translates `POST /v1/messages` into the `llm.call` IPC action. This is a new host-side component introduced in 6.5b, not 6.5a.

**Event translation:** iterate the `AsyncGenerator<SDKMessage>`. `SDKAssistantMessage` with text content → `event.stream-chunk`. `SDKResultMessage` → `event.turn-end` + `event.chat-end` on final result.

## Tool registry

### `ToolDescriptor` shape

```ts
type ToolDescriptor = {
  name: string;              // e.g., 'bash', 'memory.recall'
  description: string;
  inputSchema: JsonSchema;   // JSON Schema Draft 2020-12
  executesIn: 'sandbox' | 'host';
}
```

### Registration flow

1. Plugin declares `calls: ['tool:register']` in its manifest.
2. In its `init()`, plugin calls `bus.call('tool:register', { ...descriptor })` for each tool it contributes.
3. Boot-time cycle detector ensures `@ax/tool-dispatcher` inits first (it registers `tool:register`).
4. For `executesIn: 'host'` tools, the same plugin also registers `tool:execute:${name}` service hook.

### Catalog delivery

At sandbox startup, runner calls `ipcCall('tool.list')` → receives the full catalog → builds its internal dispatcher.

Cache-once-per-session is fine; tool set is immutable for a session's lifetime (invariant: no runtime tool registration). If future work needs dynamic tools, the runner polls or the host pushes via the inbox.

### Why not one-plugin-per-tool

`@ax/memory-cortex` will contribute 2–3 tools (store, recall, forget) plus subscriber hooks plus service hooks (internal). Splitting each tool into its own plugin means N manifests, N packages, N test suites for one conceptual unit. The `registers: ['tool:execute:memory.store', 'tool:execute:memory.recall', 'memory:record']` manifest list is the clean path — plugins group related capabilities.

---

## Workspace coordination contract

Full implementation is Week 7–9 (`@ax/workspace-git`). The **contract** ships in 6.5a via the IPC protocol so Week 7–9 consumers land against a stable shape.

### Flow

1. Agent in sandbox modifies files via tools. Sandbox-side git is local.
2. At turn end (or per-tool-call; runner-specific), sandbox commits locally with a synthetic commit message.
3. Sandbox sends `workspace.commit-notify` RPC with `{ parentVersion, commitRef, message }`.
4. Host's `@ax/workspace-git`:
   - Fetches the commit via git-http-backend from the sandbox's git dir (subprocess mode: bind-mount; k8s mode: sidecar).
   - Builds a `WorkspaceDelta` (lazy-content-fetcher shape per ax-conventions).
   - Fires `workspace:pre-apply` — subscriber chain. Any subscriber can reject.
5. If rejected: host returns `{ accepted: false, reason }`. Sandbox `git reset --hard parentVersion`; surfaces an error to the model on next turn.
6. If accepted: host promotes the commit to its authoritative store, fires `workspace:applied`, returns `{ accepted: true, version, delta }`.

### Subscribers that will hang off this contract (future slices)

- **Skill validator** (`workspace:pre-apply`) — rejects malformed skill files.
- **Canary** (`workspace:pre-apply`) — rejects commits containing secrets.
- **Skill indexer** (`workspace:applied`) — updates search index.
- **Audit** (`workspace:applied`) — logs the commit.

### Rewind semantics

The sandbox must be able to undo a rejected commit cleanly. `@ax/agent-runner-core` provides a `commitAndNotify(parentVersion, message)` helper that:
1. Does `git commit`.
2. Calls `workspace.commit-notify`.
3. On rejection: `git reset --hard parentVersion`; surfaces `WorkspaceRejectedError` to the runner's tool-loop.
4. On accept: returns the new `version` token.

Runners surface the rejection to the model as a tool error so the model can retry with corrections.

### 6.5a scope

No `@ax/workspace-git` ships. No subscribers wired. The `workspace.commit-notify` action exists in `@ax/ipc-protocol` schemas; the host's `@ax/ipc-server` returns `{ accepted: true, version: '<stub>', delta: null }` unconditionally. Agent's file edits work; no host-side diff awareness.

6.5a acceptance test does not cover workspace. Week 7–9 is when the contract earns its keep.

---

## Boundary review — the five invariants

### I1. Hook surface is transport- and storage-agnostic

- `ToolDescriptor.executesIn: 'sandbox' | 'host'` — closed set, no backend-specific values. ✅
- `WorkspaceVersion` — opaque token, no git vocabulary in payloads. ✅
- `session.next-message` — no postgres-specific fields; `cursor` is just a monotonic int. ✅
- `IPC transport` — abstracted behind `@ax/ipc-protocol`; unix socket vs cluster HTTP is a per-plugin implementation detail. ✅

### I2. No cross-plugin imports

All cross-plugin communication via hook bus (host-side) and IPC protocol (host↔sandbox). Only `@ax/core`, `@ax/ipc-protocol`, and `@ax/agent-runner-core` are shared imports.

The runner imports `@ax/agent-runner-core`; that's one-way. Runner does not import `@ax/tool-bash` (host-side plugin) — tool descriptors arrive over IPC; sandbox-local implementations live in separate `-impl` packages. ✅

### I3. No half-wired plugins

Every new 6.5a plugin is fully wired end-to-end in its shipping PR:

- `@ax/ipc-server` → called by `@ax/chat-orchestrator` → exercised in acceptance test.
- `@ax/session-inmemory` → called by `@ax/chat-orchestrator` and `@ax/ipc-server`.
- `@ax/agent-native-runner` → exercised in acceptance test.
- `@ax/llm-openrouter` → exercised in acceptance test (preset toggles to it).

No "wire this up later" packages. ✅

### I4. One source of truth per concept

- Tool catalog: `@ax/tool-dispatcher` only. Runners don't maintain their own registries.
- Session state: `@ax/session-*` only. Runner doesn't cache (reads from IPC).
- LLM credentials: host-side `@ax/llm-*` plugins. Sandbox never holds them. ✅

### I5. Capabilities explicit and minimized

- **Sandbox capabilities:** unix socket (or egress to one host URL), local filesystem scoped to workspace dir, process spawn for in-sandbox tool subprocesses. No direct DB, no cloud creds, no uncontrolled network egress.
- **IPC auth:** per-session bearer token, revocable, scoped to that session's IPC actions. Cannot impersonate another session.
- **Tool input validation:** every `tool:execute:${name}` handler validates input via Zod before execution. `tool.pre-call` subscriber chain can reject before the tool runs.
- **Untrusted content tagging:** LLM output and tool output are untrusted; `llm:pre-call` subscribers treat history content as untrusted.

**Security checklist required for every slice's PR.**
- **6.5a:** `@ax/ipc-server` is new attack surface; sandbox primitives cross the trust boundary.
- **6.5b:** `@ax/llm-openrouter` is a new third-party endpoint + new dep.
- **6.5c:** `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` and transitive deps land inside the sandbox.
- **6.5d:** `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` spawns a subprocess tree inside the sandbox; `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` is new host-side HTTP surface.

Three threat models apply throughout: sandbox escape, prompt injection, supply chain.

## Per-slice acceptance tests

### 6.5a — topology shift

End-to-end:
1. Start host process with a config that loads `@ax/sandbox-subprocess`, `@ax/session-inmemory`, `@ax/llm-anthropic`, `@ax/agent-native-runner` (via sandbox image/binary), and all tool plugins.
2. CLI sends a user message.
3. Host spawns a subprocess sandbox; runner connects over unix socket.
4. Runner polls inbox, receives user message, calls `llm.call` (routed to Anthropic).
5. LLM responds with a `bash` tool call.
6. Runner IPCs `tool.pre-call` → host fires subscribers → `allow`.
7. Runner executes bash locally in sandbox.
8. Runner IPCs `event.tool-post-call` → host fires `tool:post-call` (audit observes).
9. Runner loops back into LLM with tool result.
10. Final assistant message; runner fires `event.chat-end`; host returns outcome to CLI.

Automated test variant: mock Anthropic HTTP with a canned response chain, spawn real subprocess sandbox, assert: all IPC events fired host-side, audit plugin logged, outcome message returned. **The observable behavior of this chat must match what Week 4-6's in-process loop produced** — that's the "nothing observable changes" invariant for this slice.

### 6.5b — providers + streaming

1. Config selects `@ax/llm-openrouter` instead of `@ax/llm-anthropic`.
2. CLI sends a user message.
3. LLM responds with streaming chunks; runner emits `event.stream-chunk` per chunk.
4. CLI renders text incrementally as chunks arrive.
5. Same tool-call flow as 6.5a completes.

Automated test: mock OpenRouter HTTP with a streaming SSE response, assert `chat:stream-chunk` subscribers fire in order, final message matches.

### 6.5c — pi-session runner

1. Config sets `runner: 'pi-session'`.
2. Config sets `AX_MODEL` to any supported host provider's model (e.g., `openrouter/gpt-4.1`).
3. CLI sends a user message.
4. Sandbox starts pi-session-runner binary; pi-session runs its internal loop; LLM traffic routes through the `ax-ipc` custom api → `llm.call` IPC → host's `@ax/llm-openrouter` (or whichever is loaded).
5. Subscribers fire identically to 6.5a/6.5b (the host doesn't know pi-session is in the sandbox).

Automated test: same as 6.5a/6.5b but with pi-session runner binary. Assert: identical host-observable event sequence.

### 6.5d — claude-sdk runner

1. Config sets `runner: 'claude-sdk'`.
2. CLI sends a user message.
3. Sandbox starts claude-sdk-runner binary; SDK's `query()` runs; `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` routes LLM traffic to host's Anthropic-format proxy → `llm.call` IPC.
4. Built-in Bash tool runs inside the sandbox (subprocess tree); `canUseTool` fires `tool.pre-call` IPC; `hooks.PostToolUse` fires `event.tool-post-call` IPC.
5. MCP-registered `executesIn: 'host'` tools dispatch via `tool.execute-host` IPC.

Automated test: mock Anthropic HTTP (via the proxy), spawn real subprocess sandbox with claude-sdk-runner, assert: built-in and MCP tool calls both fire `tool:pre-call` / `tool:post-call` subscribers, outcome matches.

## Scope decisions for the 6.5a implementation plan

These are 6.5a-specific open questions. Each needs an explicit call before code lands. Later slices (6.5b–d) surface their own questions when their plans get written; the list below is scoped to the topology shift.

1. **Runner binary packaging.** Single binary per runner (bundled with tool impls), or runner binary + tool impls as dlopen-able modules? Recommendation: single binary per runner. Simpler, ships in one dockerfile COPY line. Re-examine if the binary gets large enough to matter.

2. **Auth token scheme.** Opaque random string (64 bytes base64)? JWT (signed, carries claims)? Recommendation: opaque random, verified against session store on every IPC call. JWT adds signing ceremony without clear benefit since we have the store anyway.

3. **IPC request timeouts.** Per-action or global default? v1 uses 5min for `agent_response`, 15min safety net, per-call configurable. Recommendation: per-action defaults in `@ax/ipc-protocol` (e.g., `llm.call` = 5min, `tool.pre-call` = 10s, `session.next-message` = 30s long-poll), global override in config.

4. **Concurrent IPC from sandbox.** Can multiple RPCs be in flight at once (e.g., streaming LLM chunks while issuing tool.pre-call for a queued call)? Recommendation: yes, HTTP supports it natively; runner-core uses an HTTP keep-alive connection pool. Ordering of fire-and-forget events is best-effort.

5. **Cursor semantics for `session.next-message`.** Monotonic int per session? Recommendation: int; inbox entries carry their own index; cursor = last-seen-index. Sandbox advances only after processing, so crashes replay from the last-processed cursor.

6. **Eventbus in 6.5a?** Not needed for single-host/single-replica, but `chat:stream-chunk` fan-out wants it once multi-replica arrives. Recommendation: skip for 6.5a, ship alongside `@ax/eventbus-postgres` in Week 7–9. Document the fan-out path in the design so nothing surprises us later.

7. **How much of `chat-loop.ts` survives.** The turn-loop shape ports to the sandbox-side runner. The host-side orchestrator is thinner — it spawns the sandbox, waits for chat:end, returns outcome. Recommendation: delete `chat-loop.ts` as part of 6.5a; don't leave the dead code around.

8. **ax.config.ts preset wiring.** `@ax/preset-local-native-anthropic`, `@ax/preset-local-native-openrouter`? Or a more flexible config-driven preset? Recommendation: keep current ax.config.ts loader; introduce named presets as sugar later when the combinatorial cost justifies them.

---

## Kickoff prompts for implementation-plan sessions

Each slice gets its own implementation plan, written in a fresh session after `/clear`. The plans share `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md` as the contract doc.

### 6.5a — topology shift

```
Write an implementation plan for Week 6.5a of docs/plans/2026-04-22-plugin-architecture-design.md
(agent sandboxing — topology shift). Read docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md
first — it specifies the IPC protocol, plugin decomposition, and runner contract. Scope is
strictly the topology shift: @ax/ipc-protocol, @ax/ipc-server, @ax/sandbox-subprocess extensions
(sandbox:open-session), @ax/session-inmemory, @ax/chat-orchestrator, @ax/agent-runner-core,
@ax/agent-native-runner, tool refactor (host→sandbox). Keep @ax/llm-anthropic unchanged; no
streaming; no new providers. Acceptance: Week 4-6's chat behavior is preserved, now with the
loop running in a subprocess sandbox. Invoke security-checklist for @ax/ipc-server and the
@ax/sandbox-subprocess session-lifetime extensions. Branch off the tip of Week 4-6. The plan
should be executable via subagent-driven-development.
```

### 6.5b — providers + streaming

```

docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md. Scope: @ax/llm-openrouter, streaming

```

### 6.5c — pi-session runner

Scope: @ax/agent-pi-session-runner, runner-selection config. Acceptance: swap to pi-session via
config; works with every host-side @ax/llm-* plugin without runner changes. Invoke
security-checklist for the new sandbox-side dependency tree (@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent
and its transitive deps). Branch off 6.5b. Executable via subagent-driven-development.
```

### 6.5d — claude-sdk runner

```

disable WebFetch/WebSearch/Skill/Task), the canUseTool/PostToolUse wiring, and the
Anthropic-format proxy requirement. Scope: @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner,
@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format. Acceptance: swap to claude-sdk via config; built-ins and MCP
tools both fire tool:pre-call / tool:post-call subscribers. Invoke security-checklist for
@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format (new HTTP surface) and for the claude-sdk dependency tree.
Branch off 6.5c. Executable via subagent-driven-development.
```
