content.text: Let me check the memory note about the MVP direction — there's a hint that 6.5b/c may have been dropped for MVP.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: MVP architectural direction (as of 2026-04-24)
3	description: MVP shape decided during Week 6.5a — claude-sdk runner as default, host-side MCP, scope adjustments to 6.5b/6.5c and Week 10-12
4	type: project
5	originSessionId: 9d96b989-0904-4ed7-a8be-9c18ff1345be
6	---
7	**Decision (2026-04-24):** MVP ships on `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` (6.5d) as the default runner, with MCP hosted server-side in a new `@ax/mcp-client` plugin rather than using claude-sdk's native `mcpServers`.
8	
9	**Why:**
10	- Claude-sdk's Task tool gives subagent spawning for free (no new IPC surface, no new trust boundary).
11	- `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` → `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` → `llm:call` IPC means host-side subscribers (canary, audit, compactor) still intercept every LLM call.
12	- `canUseTool` / `PreToolUse` hooks map directly to `tool:pre-call` IPC with veto + rewrite semantics.
13	- Host-side MCP (runner-agnostic) preserves the "one IPC contract, multiple runners" invariant from the 6.5 design doc — swapping in pi-session-runner or native-runner later requires no MCP rework.
14	- Host-side MCP also honors invariant 4 (one source of truth for tool catalog) and invariant 1 (tool:pre-call fires uniformly regardless of runner).
15	
16	**How to apply:**
17	- When scoping MVP work, treat `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` as the default runner. Native runner (6.5a) still exists for tests/dev.
18	- When adding new tool surfaces, default to `executesIn: 'host'` via `@ax/mcp-client` rather than runner-native integration, unless performance demands sandbox-local execution.
19	- Skip `@ax/llm-openrouter` (6.5b) for MVP — claude-sdk's Anthropic wire + proxy covers it. Revive in Week 13+ when non-Anthropic routing earns its keep.
20	- Skip `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` (6.5c) for MVP — defer to Week 13+ as "add pi-session for flexibility" candidate.
21	- Skip 6.5b streaming as a standalone slice. The web UI needs it, but fold into `@ax/channel-chat-ui` (Week 10-12) or add as a small plumb to 6.5d. Claude-sdk iterates `SDKMessage` natively.
22	- `@ax/agent-runner-core` is NOT replaceable by the SDK — it's shared sandbox-side infrastructure (IPC client, long-poll inbox, local-vs-host dispatch) that every runner wraps. Only native-runner-specific turn-loop code is replaced by claude-sdk.
23	- Workspace commits happen **at turn-end**, not per-tool-call. Single `workspace.commit-notify` per turn with aggregate diff; rollback reverts the whole turn. D4's move of redaction to `llm:pre-call` removes the per-tool-call argument. In claude-sdk runner, commit fires on `SDKResultMessage` / turn boundary, not `PostToolUse`.
24	- Memory (`@ax/memory-*`) moves OUT of Week 10-12 and into Week 13+. Use the **Strata** design (`docs/plans/memory-strata-design.md`), not legacy cortex. Strata is multi-component (observer/consolidator/retriever/promoter) and needs `@ax/scheduler` — likely two Week-13+ slices, not one.
25	
26	**Net-new work beyond existing roadmap:**
27	- `@ax/mcp-client` (new host-side plugin, slots as 6.5e or before Week 7-9)
28	- `@ax/credentials` pulled forward from Week 13+ (MCP needs it)
29	- Week 9.5 multi-tenant slice: `@ax/http-server`, `@ax/auth`, `@ax/agents`, session↔agent wiring, per-agent tool/MCP scoping, admin UI primitives (before Week 10-12 channels)
30	- Admin UI for agent + MCP CRUD lands in Week 9.5
31	- `@ax/scheduler` pulled forward from Week 13+ when Strata observer/consolidator start
32

file.content:
---
name: MVP architectural direction (as of 2026-04-24)
description: MVP shape decided during Week 6.5a — claude-sdk runner as default, host-side MCP, scope adjustments to 6.5b/6.5c and Week 10-12
type: project
originSessionId: 9d96b989-0904-4ed7-a8be-9c18ff1345be
---
**Decision (2026-04-24):** MVP ships on `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` (6.5d) as the default runner, with MCP hosted server-side in a new `@ax/mcp-client` plugin rather than using claude-sdk's native `mcpServers`.

**Why:**
- Claude-sdk's Task tool gives subagent spawning for free (no new IPC surface, no new trust boundary).
- `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` → `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` → `llm:call` IPC means host-side subscribers (canary, audit, compactor) still intercept every LLM call.
- `canUseTool` / `PreToolUse` hooks map directly to `tool:pre-call` IPC with veto + rewrite semantics.
- Host-side MCP (runner-agnostic) preserves the "one IPC contract, multiple runners" invariant from the 6.5 design doc — swapping in pi-session-runner or native-runner later requires no MCP rework.
- Host-side MCP also honors invariant 4 (one source of truth for tool catalog) and invariant 1 (tool:pre-call fires uniformly regardless of runner).

**How to apply:**
- When scoping MVP work, treat `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` as the default runner. Native runner (6.5a) still exists for tests/dev.
- When adding new tool surfaces, default to `executesIn: 'host'` via `@ax/mcp-client` rather than runner-native integration, unless performance demands sandbox-local execution.
- Skip `@ax/llm-openrouter` (6.5b) for MVP — claude-sdk's Anthropic wire + proxy covers it. Revive in Week 13+ when non-Anthropic routing earns its keep.
- Skip `@ax/agent-pi-session-runner` (6.5c) for MVP — defer to Week 13+ as "add pi-session for flexibility" candidate.
- Skip 6.5b streaming as a standalone slice. The web UI needs it, but fold into `@ax/channel-chat-ui` (Week 10-12) or add as a small plumb to 6.5d. Claude-sdk iterates `SDKMessage` natively.
- `@ax/agent-runner-core` is NOT replaceable by the SDK — it's shared sandbox-side infrastructure (IPC client, long-poll inbox, local-vs-host dispatch) that every runner wraps. Only native-runner-specific turn-loop code is replaced by claude-sdk.
- Workspace commits happen **at turn-end**, not per-tool-call. Single `workspace.commit-notify` per turn with aggregate diff; rollback reverts the whole turn. D4's move of redaction to `llm:pre-call` removes the per-tool-call argument. In claude-sdk runner, commit fires on `SDKResultMessage` / turn boundary, not `PostToolUse`.
- Memory (`@ax/memory-*`) moves OUT of Week 10-12 and into Week 13+. Use the **Strata** design (`docs/plans/memory-strata-design.md`), not legacy cortex. Strata is multi-component (observer/consolidator/retriever/promoter) and needs `@ax/scheduler` — likely two Week-13+ slices, not one.

**Net-new work beyond existing roadmap:**
- `@ax/mcp-client` (new host-side plugin, slots as 6.5e or before Week 7-9)
- `@ax/credentials` pulled forward from Week 13+ (MCP needs it)
- Week 9.5 multi-tenant slice: `@ax/http-server`, `@ax/auth`, `@ax/agents`, session↔agent wiring, per-agent tool/MCP scoping, admin UI primitives (before Week 10-12 channels)
- Admin UI for agent + MCP CRUD lands in Week 9.5
- `@ax/scheduler` pulled forward from Week 13+ when Strata observer/consolidator start

1	# Week 6.5e handoff — `@ax/mcp-client` (host-side MCP hosting)
2	
3	**For:** session starting after Week 6.5d (claude-sdk runner) lands.
4	**Previous slices:** Weeks 1–2, 3, 4–6, 6.5a (topology shift), 6.5d (claude-sdk runner).
5	**Successors:** Week 7–9 (k8s deployment) — MCP connections need to survive replica restarts; MVP admin UI (Week 9.5) — user-configurable MCP server list per agent.
6	
7	Skipped from the original 6.5 design: 6.5b (openrouter + streaming) and 6.5c (pi-session runner). See `project_mvp_direction.md` in memory for rationale.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Goal
12	
13	Add user-configurable MCP servers to AX in a way that's runner-agnostic: every runner (native, claude-sdk, future pi-session) sees MCP tools through the same `tool:register` / `tool:execute:${name}` surface as every other tool.
14	
15	Why host-side, not runner-side:
16	
17	- **Runner-agnostic by construction.** Claude-sdk has native `mcpServers` support; pi-coding-agent may or may not; our native runner doesn't. Hosting MCP clients on the host means one implementation covers all three and any future runner.
18	- **One source of truth (invariant 4).** The tool catalog lives in `@ax/tool-dispatcher`. If MCP tools live in runner-land, the catalog forks per runner.
19	- **Uniform subscriber semantics (invariant 1).** `tool:pre-call` / `tool:post-call` fire for MCP tools the same as bash. Canary, audit, memory see everything with no special-casing.
20	- **Capability minimization (invariant 5).** MCP servers run in host-land where we already have credential handling; sandbox doesn't need network egress to MCP endpoints.
21	
22	---
23	
24	## Deliverables
25	
26	- **`@ax/mcp-client`** — host-side plugin. At init, reads MCP server configs from storage. For each enabled server, opens a client connection (stdio/http/sse per MCP spec). Calls `tool:register` for each remote tool the server advertises, with `executesIn: 'host'`. Registers `tool:execute:${toolName}` service hook per tool, whose handler marshals the call over MCP and returns the result.
27	- **`@ax/credentials`** — pulled forward from Week 13+. MCP servers need API keys / OAuth tokens. One credentials plugin unlocks MCP + later audit exporters + OAuth flows. Minimal shape: `credentials:get(key) → value` service hook backed by `storage:set/get` with at-rest encryption.
28	- **`@ax/mcp-admin-api`** *(or folded into a larger admin plugin later)* — registers HTTP routes on `@ax/http-server` for CRUD on MCP server configs. Requires Week 10–12's `@ax/http-server` to exist, OR ships a minimal standalone HTTP listener for pre-channel use. Decide below.
29	
30	---
31	
32	## Scope decisions to make while writing the plan
33	
34	1. **MCP client library.** Use `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (official TypeScript SDK) rather than hand-rolling. It handles stdio/http/sse transports and the JSON-RPC layer.
35	
36	2. **Transport set at MVP.** Support which?
37	   - **(a)** Stdio only (simplest, covers most community MCP servers).
38	   - **(b)** Stdio + HTTP + SSE.
39	   - **Recommendation:** (b). HTTP/SSE lets users bring remote MCP services without needing them co-located. The SDK gives us all three essentially free.
40	
41	3. **MCP server lifecycle.** Stdio servers are child processes of the host. When?
42	   - **(a)** At plugin init (eager) — all configured servers spawn at host boot.
43	   - **(b)** Lazy — spawn on first tool call, keep alive with an idle timer.
44	   - **(c)** Per-session — spawn when a chat that uses the server starts, tear down on `chat:end`.
45	   - **Recommendation:** (a) for MVP. Eager simplifies the tool catalog (known at boot), avoids cold-start latency on first call, and matches how the architecture already handles the tool registration phase. Revisit if a heavy/slow MCP server makes boot painful.
46	
47	4. **Per-agent MCP scoping.** Feature 2 says "users configure MCP connections in admin interface." Feature 6 says "personal vs team agents." So MCP configs belong to agents, not to the global host.
48	   - Implications: the tool catalog is per-agent-session, not global. `tool:list` fired during `sandbox:open-session` needs to filter to the calling agent's MCP allowlist.
49	   - This changes `@ax/tool-dispatcher`'s current "one global catalog" assumption — the dispatcher needs to maintain per-agent filtered views.
50	   - **Recommendation:** punt agent-scoping to Week 9.5 (when `@ax/agents` lands). For MVP `@ax/mcp-client` ships with global scope: all MCP servers visible to all sessions. Clearly flagged as temporary. Week 9.5 adds the per-agent filter.
51	
52	5. **Credential storage.** MCP server configs include secrets (API keys for HTTP/SSE servers, sometimes env vars for stdio servers).
53	   - Store encrypted at rest via `@ax/credentials`. Reference by credential ID from the MCP server config, not inline value.
54	   - Encryption key source: env var (`AX_CREDENTIALS_KEY`) for MVP. KMS integration is a Week 13+ candidate.
55	
56	6. **Dead MCP server handling.** Stdio server crashes, HTTP endpoint goes down.
57	   - Mark server unhealthy; its tools become temporarily unavailable.
58	   - `tool:execute:${name}` returns a structured error (`MCP_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE`) that the runner surfaces to the model as a tool error, not a chat-terminating failure.
59	   - Reconnect with backoff.
60	
61	7. **Admin API surface.** Minimal CRUD:
62	   - `POST /admin/mcp-servers` — create config (name, transport, command/url, env, credential refs).
63	   - `GET /admin/mcp-servers` — list.
64	   - `PATCH /admin/mcp-servers/:id` — update, enable/disable.
65	   - `DELETE /admin/mcp-servers/:id`.
66	   - `POST /admin/mcp-servers/:id/test` — attempt connection, return status + discovered tools.
67	   - Auth: this surface requires admin identity. Since `@ax/auth` is Week 9.5, MVP can either (a) punt auth on the admin API to Week 9.5 and use a bootstrap bearer token, or (b) ship a minimal bearer-token config read from env. **Recommendation:** (b) — one env-var admin token for pre-9.5, replaced by real auth in 9.5.
68	
69	---
70	
71	## Boundary review
72	
73	- **`tool:register` payload:** already specs `{ name, description, inputSchema, executesIn }`. MCP tools use this unchanged. ✅
74	- **`tool:execute:${name}` service hook:** generic service hook per tool name, already part of the dispatcher design. MCP tools register via `tool:register` then implement the corresponding service hook. ✅
75	- **New payload fields in admin API:** `transport: 'stdio' | 'http' | 'sse'` — closed set, no backend-specific leak. `credential_ref` — opaque reference, no credential storage vocabulary leaks. ✅
76	- **Alternative impl this hook surface could have:** any other dynamic-tool-source plugin (e.g., a future OpenAPI-to-tool plugin, a plugin that registers a fixed set of LLM-as-tool calls). The `tool:register` + `tool:execute:${name}` pair isn't MCP-specific, which is the point. ✅
77	
78	## Security — `security-checklist` required (heavy)
79	
80	Three threat models all apply:
81	
82	- **Sandbox escape:** N/A directly — MCP runs host-side, not in the sandbox. But an MCP server returning malicious tool output is prompt injection into the model running in the sandbox.
83	- **Prompt injection:** MCP server output is **third-party model-fed input**. Treat as untrusted at every hop. `tool:post-call` subscribers (canary) must scan MCP output the same as bash output. Document in `@ax/mcp-client`'s SECURITY.md that MCP servers can be hostile.
84	- **Supply chain:** `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` is a new dependency. Community MCP servers are arbitrary npm packages or remote services — users bring their own. The plugin never auto-installs MCP servers; configs reference already-available commands or URLs.
85	
86	Additional concerns:
87	- **Credential exposure.** `@ax/credentials` must never log decrypted values. Redact in error paths.
88	- **Stdio MCP process isolation.** Stdio MCP servers run as host subprocesses. Consider ulimits / resource caps, especially for community servers.
89	- **HTTP MCP TLS.** HTTP transport must default to HTTPS; log a warning for plain-HTTP configs. No cert pinning at MVP but note it as Week 13+.
90	- **Input schema handling.** MCP servers declare their own JSON Schema for tool inputs. We pass this through to the model via `tool:list`. Malicious schemas could confuse the model or embed prompt-injection hints in descriptions. Render tool descriptions as untrusted content, not trusted system text.
91	
92	## Acceptance test
93	
94	Automated:
95	1. Start host with one stdio MCP server config (e.g., a minimal test MCP server included in `@ax/test-harness`).
96	2. Verify `@ax/mcp-client` spawned the subprocess and called `tool:register` for each advertised tool.
97	3. Run a chat that causes the model to call one of the MCP tools.
98	4. Assert `tool:pre-call` fired, `tool:execute:${name}` dispatched to the MCP client, call round-tripped through MCP wire protocol, response returned.
99	5. Assert `tool:post-call` fired with the result visible.
100	6. Kill the MCP subprocess mid-test; verify next call returns `MCP_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE` error without terminating the chat.
101	
102	Manual: configure a real community MCP server (e.g., `mcp-server-filesystem` in read-only mode), run a chat that uses it, verify tools appear in the catalog and execute correctly.
103	
104	## Out of scope / deferred
105	
106	- Per-agent MCP filtering → Week 9.5.
107	- OAuth flows for MCP servers that require them → Week 13+ (when a user asks).
108	- Dynamic MCP server reload (config changes without restart) → Week 13+.
109	- MCP server sandboxing beyond subprocess isolation → Week 13+.
110	- KMS-backed credential encryption → Week 13+.
111	
112	## Kickoff prompt for next session
113	
114	After `/clear`:
115	
116	```
117	Write an implementation plan for Week 6.5e (@ax/mcp-client + @ax/credentials). Read
118	docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5e-mcp-client-handoff.md first — it specifies host-side MCP
119	as runner-agnostic by construction, punts per-agent scoping to Week 9.5, and flags the
120	three threat models. Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk for transport. Invoke security-checklist
121	for @ax/mcp-client (untrusted third-party content + new supply-chain dep) and @ax/credentials
122	(at-rest encryption). Branch off the tip of 6.5d. Executable via subagent-driven-development.
123	```

file.content:
# Week 6.5e handoff — `@ax/mcp-client` (host-side MCP hosting)

**For:** session starting after Week 6.5d (claude-sdk runner) lands.
**Previous slices:** Weeks 1–2, 3, 4–6, 6.5a (topology shift), 6.5d (claude-sdk runner).
**Successors:** Week 7–9 (k8s deployment) — MCP connections need to survive replica restarts; MVP admin UI (Week 9.5) — user-configurable MCP server list per agent.

Skipped from the original 6.5 design: 6.5b (openrouter + streaming) and 6.5c (pi-session runner). See `project_mvp_direction.md` in memory for rationale.

---

## Goal

Add user-configurable MCP servers to AX in a way that's runner-agnostic: every runner (native, claude-sdk, future pi-session) sees MCP tools through the same `tool:register` / `tool:execute:${name}` surface as every other tool.

Why host-side, not runner-side:

- **Runner-agnostic by construction.** Claude-sdk has native `mcpServers` support; pi-coding-agent may or may not; our native runner doesn't. Hosting MCP clients on the host means one implementation covers all three and any future runner.
- **One source of truth (invariant 4).** The tool catalog lives in `@ax/tool-dispatcher`. If MCP tools live in runner-land, the catalog forks per runner.
- **Uniform subscriber semantics (invariant 1).** `tool:pre-call` / `tool:post-call` fire for MCP tools the same as bash. Canary, audit, memory see everything with no special-casing.
- **Capability minimization (invariant 5).** MCP servers run in host-land where we already have credential handling; sandbox doesn't need network egress to MCP endpoints.

---

## Deliverables

- **`@ax/mcp-client`** — host-side plugin. At init, reads MCP server configs from storage. For each enabled server, opens a client connection (stdio/http/sse per MCP spec). Calls `tool:register` for each remote tool the server advertises, with `executesIn: 'host'`. Registers `tool:execute:${toolName}` service hook per tool, whose handler marshals the call over MCP and returns the result.
- **`@ax/credentials`** — pulled forward from Week 13+. MCP servers need API keys / OAuth tokens. One credentials plugin unlocks MCP + later audit exporters + OAuth flows. Minimal shape: `credentials:get(key) → value` service hook backed by `storage:set/get` with at-rest encryption.
- **`@ax/mcp-admin-api`** *(or folded into a larger admin plugin later)* — registers HTTP routes on `@ax/http-server` for CRUD on MCP server configs. Requires Week 10–12's `@ax/http-server` to exist, OR ships a minimal standalone HTTP listener for pre-channel use. Decide below.

---

## Scope decisions to make while writing the plan

1. **MCP client library.** Use `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (official TypeScript SDK) rather than hand-rolling. It handles stdio/http/sse transports and the JSON-RPC layer.

2. **Transport set at MVP.** Support which?
   - **(a)** Stdio only (simplest, covers most community MCP servers).
   - **(b)** Stdio + HTTP + SSE.
   - **Recommendation:** (b). HTTP/SSE lets users bring remote MCP services without needing them co-located. The SDK gives us all three essentially free.

3. **MCP server lifecycle.** Stdio servers are child processes of the host. When?
   - **(a)** At plugin init (eager) — all configured servers spawn at host boot.
   - **(b)** Lazy — spawn on first tool call, keep alive with an idle timer.
   - **(c)** Per-session — spawn when a chat that uses the server starts, tear down on `chat:end`.
   - **Recommendation:** (a) for MVP. Eager simplifies the tool catalog (known at boot), avoids cold-start latency on first call, and matches how the architecture already handles the tool registration phase. Revisit if a heavy/slow MCP server makes boot painful.

4. **Per-agent MCP scoping.** Feature 2 says "users configure MCP connections in admin interface." Feature 6 says "personal vs team agents." So MCP configs belong to agents, not to the global host.
   - Implications: the tool catalog is per-agent-session, not global. `tool:list` fired during `sandbox:open-session` needs to filter to the calling agent's MCP allowlist.
   - This changes `@ax/tool-dispatcher`'s current "one global catalog" assumption — the dispatcher needs to maintain per-agent filtered views.
   - **Recommendation:** punt agent-scoping to Week 9.5 (when `@ax/agents` lands). For MVP `@ax/mcp-client` ships with global scope: all MCP servers visible to all sessions. Clearly flagged as temporary. Week 9.5 adds the per-agent filter.

5. **Credential storage.** MCP server configs include secrets (API keys for HTTP/SSE servers, sometimes env vars for stdio servers).
   - Store encrypted at rest via `@ax/credentials`. Reference by credential ID from the MCP server config, not inline value.
   - Encryption key source: env var (`AX_CREDENTIALS_KEY`) for MVP. KMS integration is a Week 13+ candidate.

6. **Dead MCP server handling.** Stdio server crashes, HTTP endpoint goes down.
   - Mark server unhealthy; its tools become temporarily unavailable.
   - `tool:execute:${name}` returns a structured error (`MCP_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE`) that the runner surfaces to the model as a tool error, not a chat-terminating failure.
   - Reconnect with backoff.

7. **Admin API surface.** Minimal CRUD:
   - `POST /admin/mcp-servers` — create config (name, transport, command/url, env, credential refs).
   - `GET /admin/mcp-servers` — list.
   - `PATCH /admin/mcp-servers/:id` — update, enable/disable.
   - `DELETE /admin/mcp-servers/:id`.
   - `POST /admin/mcp-servers/:id/test` — attempt connection, return status + discovered tools.
   - Auth: this surface requires admin identity. Since `@ax/auth` is Week 9.5, MVP can either (a) punt auth on the admin API to Week 9.5 and use a bootstrap bearer token, or (b) ship a minimal bearer-token config read from env. **Recommendation:** (b) — one env-var admin token for pre-9.5, replaced by real auth in 9.5.

---

## Boundary review

- **`tool:register` payload:** already specs `{ name, description, inputSchema, executesIn }`. MCP tools use this unchanged. ✅
- **`tool:execute:${name}` service hook:** generic service hook per tool name, already part of the dispatcher design. MCP tools register via `tool:register` then implement the corresponding service hook. ✅
- **New payload fields in admin API:** `transport: 'stdio' | 'http' | 'sse'` — closed set, no backend-specific leak. `credential_ref` — opaque reference, no credential storage vocabulary leaks. ✅
- **Alternative impl this hook surface could have:** any other dynamic-tool-source plugin (e.g., a future OpenAPI-to-tool plugin, a plugin that registers a fixed set of LLM-as-tool calls). The `tool:register` + `tool:execute:${name}` pair isn't MCP-specific, which is the point. ✅

## Security — `security-checklist` required (heavy)

Three threat models all apply:

- **Sandbox escape:** N/A directly — MCP runs host-side, not in the sandbox. But an MCP server returning malicious tool output is prompt injection into the model running in the sandbox.
- **Prompt injection:** MCP server output is **third-party model-fed input**. Treat as untrusted at every hop. `tool:post-call` subscribers (canary) must scan MCP output the same as bash output. Document in `@ax/mcp-client`'s SECURITY.md that MCP servers can be hostile.
- **Supply chain:** `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` is a new dependency. Community MCP servers are arbitrary npm packages or remote services — users bring their own. The plugin never auto-installs MCP servers; configs reference already-available commands or URLs.

Additional concerns:
- **Credential exposure.** `@ax/credentials` must never log decrypted values. Redact in error paths.
- **Stdio MCP process isolation.** Stdio MCP servers run as host subprocesses. Consider ulimits / resource caps, especially for community servers.
- **HTTP MCP TLS.** HTTP transport must default to HTTPS; log a warning for plain-HTTP configs. No cert pinning at MVP but note it as Week 13+.
- **Input schema handling.** MCP servers declare their own JSON Schema for tool inputs. We pass this through to the model via `tool:list`. Malicious schemas could confuse the model or embed prompt-injection hints in descriptions. Render tool descriptions as untrusted content, not trusted system text.

## Acceptance test

Automated:
1. Start host with one stdio MCP server config (e.g., a minimal test MCP server included in `@ax/test-harness`).
2. Verify `@ax/mcp-client` spawned the subprocess and called `tool:register` for each advertised tool.
3. Run a chat that causes the model to call one of the MCP tools.
4. Assert `tool:pre-call` fired, `tool:execute:${name}` dispatched to the MCP client, call round-tripped through MCP wire protocol, response returned.
5. Assert `tool:post-call` fired with the result visible.
6. Kill the MCP subprocess mid-test; verify next call returns `MCP_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE` error without terminating the chat.

Manual: configure a real community MCP server (e.g., `mcp-server-filesystem` in read-only mode), run a chat that uses it, verify tools appear in the catalog and execute correctly.

## Out of scope / deferred

- Per-agent MCP filtering → Week 9.5.
- OAuth flows for MCP servers that require them → Week 13+ (when a user asks).
- Dynamic MCP server reload (config changes without restart) → Week 13+.
- MCP server sandboxing beyond subprocess isolation → Week 13+.
- KMS-backed credential encryption → Week 13+.

## Kickoff prompt for next session

After `/clear`:

```
Write an implementation plan for Week 6.5e (@ax/mcp-client + @ax/credentials). Read
docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5e-mcp-client-handoff.md first — it specifies host-side MCP
as runner-agnostic by construction, punts per-agent scoping to Week 9.5, and flags the
three threat models. Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk for transport. Invoke security-checklist
for @ax/mcp-client (untrusted third-party content + new supply-chain dep) and @ax/credentials
(at-rest encryption). Branch off the tip of 6.5d. Executable via subagent-driven-development.
```

Let me gather remaining context in parallel, then flag this and write the plan.

input.content:
# Week 6.5d — claude-sdk runner Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Ship `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` (sandbox-side) + `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` (host-side) so AX can swap the sandbox runner to `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` via config, with claude-sdk's built-in tools AND host-mediated tools both firing the existing `tool:pre-call` / `tool:post-call` subscribers through the unchanged IPC contract.

**Architecture:**

- The runner wraps `query()` from `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. Built-in tools (`Bash`, `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `TodoWrite`) stay enabled and run inside the sandbox process tree (claude-sdk spawns subprocess descendants); `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `Skill`, `Task` are disabled via `disallowedTools`. Any `executesIn: 'host'` tool from `tool.list` is surfaced to the SDK through an in-process `createSdkMcpServer` whose handlers forward to `tool.execute-host` IPC.
- `canUseTool` callback issues `tool.pre-call` IPC (veto + rewrite); `hooks.PostToolUse` issues `event.tool-post-call` IPC (observation-only per D4). Works uniformly for built-ins and SDK-MCP tools.
- LLM traffic routes via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` → host-side `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` → `llm:call` service hook. The proxy translates Anthropic Messages API wire format (`POST /v1/messages` with multi-block content, `tool_use`/`tool_result`, system string, tools array, streaming SSE) into `@ax/core`'s flat `LlmRequest` shape and back. Session bearer token is reused as `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`; the proxy verifies it via `session:resolve-token`.
- No cross-plugin imports (I2). Schema lives in `@ax/ipc-protocol` (unchanged — 6.5d adds **no** new IPC actions; host-mediated tools reuse `tool.execute-host`).

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, pnpm workspace, Zod schemas, Node `http` (proxy listener), `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` (sandbox-side), vitest, existing `@ax/agent-runner-core` + `@ax/ipc-protocol` unchanged.

**Branch:** `feat/week-6.5d-claude-sdk-runner` off `main` (tip of 6.5a, which is `f07a6cf`). The kickoff prompt said "branch off 6.5c," but 6.5b/6.5c are skipped for MVP per `project_mvp_direction.md`. Flag this to reviewer in the PR description.

---

## Prior-slice deviations folded into invariants

6.5a (PR #6) surfaced the following lessons; 6.5d carries them forward as numbered invariants the reviewer can audit against.

- **I1 — runner holds no LLM credentials.** The sandbox never sees `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in the real sense. The env var passed to claude-sdk is the per-session bearer token (same token that authenticates IPC). The proxy verifies it via `session:resolve-token`, same path `@ax/ipc-server` uses.
- **I2 — schema source of truth stays in `@ax/ipc-protocol`.** 6.5d adds NO new IPC actions. `tool.execute-host` (already defined, schema-only in 6.5a) earns its runtime wiring here. If a field needs adding, add it in `@ax/ipc-protocol` in one task and consume it in the next.
- **I3 — proxy HTTP surface is a new attack surface.** Bind to `127.0.0.1` only, emit `Authorization: Bearer` check BEFORE any body parsing, enforce body-size cap identical to `@ax/ipc-server` (4 MiB), redact token in logs. Security-checklist below is mandatory.
- **I4 — no half-wired plugins.** Both packages are wired through to the acceptance test in the same PR. The runner binary is resolvable from `@ax/cli` via `createRequire` (same mechanism as `@ax/agent-native-runner`).
- **I5 — honest tool name passthrough.** The claude-sdk tool name (`Bash`, `Read`, …) is what flows to `tool:pre-call` subscribers. Do NOT remap to `bash` / `read_file` — that would forge the audit trail, and pi-session runner (Week 13+) would need its own remap. Subscribers that care about a specific tool handle the naming difference themselves.
- **I6 — no new dependency on `@ax/tool-bash-impl` / `@ax/tool-file-io-impl`.** claude-sdk ships its own Bash/Read/Write/Edit. The sandbox-side impl packages stay wired to `@ax/agent-native-runner` only; the claude-sdk runner does not import them.
- **I7 — turn boundary = `SDKResultMessage`.** `event.turn-end` and `event.chat-end` fire on the SDK's terminal result, never mid-stream. Workspace commits (when Week 7–9 ships them) happen here; do not emit them per-tool.
- **I8 — streaming SSE from the proxy is synthesized, not pass-through.** IPC `llm.call` is non-streaming. The proxy issues one IPC call per `/v1/messages` request and then emits faux SSE frames (`message_start`, `content_block_start`, `content_block_delta`, `content_block_stop`, `message_delta`, `message_stop`) to satisfy claude-code CLI's streaming expectations. No partial-token fan-out in 6.5d; 6.5b-style streaming is Week 13+.
- **I9 — proxy listener lifecycle mirrors IPC listener.** Per-session bind (ephemeral port on 127.0.0.1), started alongside `ipc:start` and torn down alongside `ipc:stop`. Host-wide singleton is a k8s concern (Week 7–9), not MVP.

---

## Scope decisions this plan locks in

Surfaced by the design doc for 6.5d; each needs an explicit call before code lands.

1. **Streaming vs non-streaming proxy.** Support BOTH. claude-code CLI defaults to `stream: true`; if we ship non-streaming only we lose the built-in. Non-streaming path: direct bulk JSON response translated from one `llm:call`. Streaming path: one `llm:call` IPC, synthesize SSE frames from the bulk response. Token-level streaming from the provider is NOT re-streamed (that's 6.5b work we skipped).

2. **Tool name convention.** Pass the claude-sdk tool name verbatim to `tool:pre-call` and `tool:post-call`. MCP tools reaching claude-sdk via `createSdkMcpServer` carry the `mcp__ax-host-tools__<toolname>` prefix the SDK synthesizes; our `tool:pre-call` subscriber receives this prefixed name. The `executeToolHost` IPC dispatch inside the runner strips the prefix before calling `tool.execute-host` (host expects the bare tool name).

3. **MCP server grouping.** One `createSdkMcpServer({ name: 'ax-host-tools', tools: [...] })` per runner. All `executesIn: 'host'` tools from `tool.list` register into this one server. Keeps the tool namespace flat and the SDK surface predictable.

4. **Disabled built-ins.** `disallowedTools: ['WebFetch', 'WebSearch', 'Skill', 'Task']`. Do NOT use `allowedTools` (whitelist) — that would require re-enumerating every SDK-MCP tool and is fragile when the SDK adds new built-ins.

5. **Settings sources.** `settingSources: []` (claude-sdk-docs default). We do NOT want CLAUDE.md / project settings bleeding into the sandbox session. AX owns the context.

6. **System prompt.** `systemPrompt: { type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code' }`. The runner's job is to be claude-sdk; reinventing the system prompt here would defeat the point. Future: allow CLI config to append.

7. **`pathToClaudeCodeExecutable`.** Leave unset. The SDK bundles its own claude-code CLI. Documented side effect: the sandbox `node_modules` must contain `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` at runtime — covered by the runner package's dependency and pnpm's hoisting.

8. **Proxy auth scheme.** Reuse the existing session bearer token. No new auth boundary. The proxy calls `session:resolve-token` to validate — same check `@ax/ipc-server` does.

9. **Proxy transport.** Plain HTTP on `127.0.0.1:<ephemeral>` per session, URL injected via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:<port>`. The sandbox subprocess inherits from the host's network namespace so this works. k8s / Docker sandbox plugins will have to reintroduce routing (Week 7–9); the plan documents this as a known extension point.

10. **Built-in vs MCP `tool:pre-call` parity.** Per design doc D4 + the acceptance test: every tool call — built-in or MCP — must fire `tool:pre-call` with a `ToolCall` that has `{id, name, input}` and respect the verdict. `canUseTool` gives us `toolName` + `input` only (no tool_use_id), so we synthesize a runner-local uuid for the IPC call. PostToolUse gets the real tool_use_id; we emit that as the `id` on `event.tool-post-call`. Subscribers correlating pre↔post by id should not (per D4, post is observation-only); we do not expose a correlation contract in 6.5d.

---

## Bite-sized tasks

Each task ends with a test + a commit. Frequent commits. DRY. YAGNI.

### Task 1: Branch + package scaffolding

**Files:**
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/package.json`
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/tsconfig.json`
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/index.ts`
- Create: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/package.json`
- Create: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/tsconfig.json`
- Create: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts`
- Modify: `pnpm-workspace.yaml` (no change if using `packages/*` glob — confirm)
- Modify: `tsconfig.json` (refs)

**Step 1: Create branch.**

```bash
git checkout main && git pull && git checkout -b feat/week-6.5d-claude-sdk-runner
```

**Step 2: Scaffold `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` package.json.**

Match `@ax/ipc-server`'s shape (ESM, tsc build, vitest test). Dependencies: `@ax/core` (workspace), `@ax/ipc-protocol` (workspace), `zod`. No runtime dependency on `@ax/session-inmemory` — coordinate via hooks (I2).

**Step 3: Scaffold `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` package.json.**

Match `@ax/agent-native-runner`'s shape (binary via `bin` field, tsc build). Dependencies: `@ax/agent-runner-core` (workspace), `@ax/ipc-protocol` (workspace), `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` (latest), `zod`. NO dep on `@ax/tool-bash-impl` / `@ax/tool-file-io-impl` (I6).

**Step 4: Write placeholder `src/index.ts` and `src/main.ts` that compile and `process.exit(2)` respectively.**

**Step 5: Update workspace TS refs.**

Add both packages to `tsconfig.json`'s `references`. `pnpm install` from repo root. `pnpm build` compiles with empty packages.

**Step 6: Commit.**

```bash
git add packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner tsconfig.json
git commit -m "scaffold(6.5d): @ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format + @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner skeletons"
```

---

### Task 2: Anthropic-format ↔ LlmRequest translation (proxy, pure functions)

Do this FIRST, before any HTTP. Pure functions are test-driven cleanly.

**Files:**
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/translate-request.ts`
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/translate-response.ts`
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/anthropic-schemas.ts`
- Test: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/__tests__/translate-request.test.ts`
- Test: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/__tests__/translate-response.test.ts`

**Step 1: Write failing request-translation tests.**

Translate `{model, max_tokens, system?, messages: ContentBlock[], tools?, stream?}` → `LlmRequest` from `@ax/core` (`{messages: ChatMessage[], tools?: ToolDescriptor[]}`). Tests cover:

- Plain user/assistant strings → `ChatMessage[]` with role/content.
- Multi-block content `[{type:'text', text:'hi'}, {type:'image', ...}]` → concatenate text, drop image blocks (log warn). Image support is Week 13+.
- `tool_use` block in an assistant message → synthesize an assistant ChatMessage whose content is `'[tool_use <name>] <JSON.stringify(input)>'` (matches the Week 4–6 wire contract Task 15 asserts in `@ax/agent-native-runner`). Document the lossy nature.
- `tool_result` block in a user message → synthesize a user ChatMessage with `'[tool <name>] <result text>'` — same verbatim format the native turn loop emits.
- `system: string` → prepend as a `role: 'system'` ChatMessage (llm-anthropic maps system back to top-level `system` on its way out, round-trip is lossy-but-symmetric).
- `tools: AnthropicToolSpec[]` → map to `ToolDescriptor[]` with `executesIn: 'host'` set to a sentinel — the proxy doesn't know the host/sandbox flag; it's only forwarding the LLM's visible catalog. Document sentinel = `'host'` because the LLM's view is the HOST'S view, and the proxy doesn't decide where execution happens.

Run: `pnpm --filter @ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format test translate-request -v`
Expected: FAIL (functions don't exist).

**Step 2: Define `anthropic-schemas.ts` with Zod schemas for the request/response wire shape.**

Scope: the subset we actually consume. Unknown optional fields passed through / ignored. `.passthrough()` so SDK-side additions don't break us.

**Step 3: Implement `translate-request.ts`.**

**Step 4: Green request tests.**

**Step 5: Write failing response-translation tests.**

Translate `LlmResponse` (`{assistantMessage, toolCalls, usage?}`) → Anthropic `Messages.Message` (`{id, type:'message', role:'assistant', model, stop_reason, content: ContentBlock[], usage}`). Tests cover:

- Pure text response → one `{type:'text', text}` block.
- Text + tool calls → text block(s) followed by `tool_use` blocks with `{id, name, input}`.
- Empty text + tool calls → only tool_use blocks.
- Stop reasons: 'end_turn' when no tool calls, 'tool_use' when any tool calls present.
- Usage passthrough when present; omitted when missing.

**Step 6: Implement `translate-response.ts`.**

```bash

```

1. Emits `event: message_start\ndata: {...}\n\n` with `message.id` + empty content.

Event names and field names must match the Anthropic Messages API streaming spec verbatim (cross-reference: `@anthropic-ai/sdk` streaming SSE docs). A mismatch here is what breaks claude-code CLI.

### Task 4: Proxy HTTP listener + plugin registration

**Files:**
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/listener.ts`

**Step 1: Write failing listener integration tests.**

Spin up the listener on port 0 (ephemeral), issue a `POST /v1/messages` with a real Anthropic-format payload, assert:

- Non-streaming (no `stream: true`): responds `200 application/json` with valid Anthropic `Message` shape.
- Streaming: responds `200 text/event-stream` with the expected sequence of events.
- Missing `Authorization: Bearer <token>` → `401`.
- Invalid token → `401` (mocked `session:resolve-token` → reject).
- Body > 4 MiB → `413`.
- Request-level errors in `llm:call` → `502 application/json` error envelope.

Use a fake `HookBus` that stubs `session:resolve-token` (returns `{sessionId}`) and `llm:call` (returns a canned `LlmResponse`).

**Step 2: Implement listener.**

- Bind to `127.0.0.1:0`; return `{ port, close }` handle.
- Auth check BEFORE body read (I3).
- Reuse the body-cap primitives from `@ax/ipc-server` if extractable, or redeclare the `MAX_FRAME` ceiling (4 * 1024 * 1024) — the sandbox-side equivalent in `@ax/agent-runner-core` already does the latter.
- Route: `POST /v1/messages` only. Everything else → `404`.
- Parse body → `AnthropicRequestSchema.safeParse`; error → `400` with `{ type: 'error', error: { type: 'invalid_request_error', message } }`.
- Call `bus.call('llm:call', ctx, translateRequest(parsed))`.
- If `stream: true`: write SSE frames. Else: JSON bulk response.

**Step 3: Implement plugin.**

- Manifest: `registers: ['llm-proxy:start', 'llm-proxy:stop']`, `calls: ['session:resolve-token', 'llm:call']`.
- `init()`: register the two service hooks. `start({sessionId})` returns `{ url, port }` after binding. `stop({sessionId})` closes and removes the entry.
- Session indexing: a `Map<sessionId, ListenerHandle>` owned by the plugin.
- **Ctx construction for `llm:call`:** reconstruct a `ChatContext` from the session-id. For MVP, call `session:resolve-token` returning `{sessionId, agentId?, userId?}`, then `makeChatContext({...})`. Future: have `@ax/session-inmemory` expose a richer `session:get-context` hook. For 6.5d, fabricating a minimal ctx is fine (logger defaults to a no-op).

**Step 4: Wire `sandbox:open-session` to the proxy.**

Modify `packages/sandbox-subprocess/src/open-session.ts` AND update its declared `calls` in `plugin.ts`:

- Add `calls: ['llm-proxy:start', 'llm-proxy:stop']` to the manifest.
- After `ipc:start` succeeds, call `bus.call('llm-proxy:start', ctx, { sessionId: created.sessionId })` → capture `{ url }`.
- Inject `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL=<url>` into the runner's `sessionEnv` map.
- On child close, also call `llm-proxy:stop`.

**Note:** this couples `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` to the new hook. Boundary-review: it's a generic `llm-proxy:*` hook surface. Alternate impl = a mock proxy for tests or a different wire format (OpenAI, Gemini) in the future. ✅ no leaky names.

**Step 5: Green the listener + plugin tests.**

```bash
pnpm --filter @ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format test -v
pnpm --filter @ax/sandbox-subprocess test -v
```
Expected: PASS.

**Step 6: Commit.**

```bash
git commit -am "feat(llm-proxy): HTTP listener + plugin + sandbox-subprocess env wiring"
```

### Task 10: Wire `query()` — runner main entry point

```ts
import { query } from '@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk';
import {
  createIpcClient,
  createInboxLoop,
} from '@ax/agent-runner-core';
import type {
  ChatMessage,

} from '@ax/ipc-protocol';
import { readRunnerEnv } from './env.js';
import { createCanUseTool } from './can-use-tool.js';
import { createPostToolUseHook } from './post-tool-use.js';
import { createHostMcpServer } from './host-mcp-server.js';

const DISABLED_BUILTINS = ['WebFetch', 'WebSearch', 'Skill', 'Task'] as const;

export async function main(): Promise<number> {
  const env = readRunnerEnv();
  const client = createIpcClient({
    socketPath: env.ipcSocket,
    token: env.authToken,
  });

  const { tools } = (await client.call('tool.list', {})) as ToolListResponse;
  const hostMcpServer = createHostMcpServer({ client, tools });

  const inbox = createInboxLoop({ client });
  const history: ChatMessage[] = [];

  try {
    for (;;) {
      const entry = await inbox.next();
      if (entry.type === 'cancel') break;
      if (entry.payload === undefined) continue;

      const userText = entry.payload.content;

      // claude-sdk's `query()` accepts string OR async iterable. We use a
      // string per call since AX's inbox model is one-user-message-per-turn.
      // `continue: true` on subsequent calls reuses the prior session.
      const queryIter = query({
        prompt: userText,
        options: {
          env: {
            ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL: env.llmProxyUrl,
            ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: env.authToken,
          },
          cwd: env.workspaceRoot,
          disallowedTools: [...DISABLED_BUILTINS],
          canUseTool: createCanUseTool({ client }),
          hooks: {
            PostToolUse: [
              { hooks: [createPostToolUseHook({ client })] },
            ],
          },
          mcpServers: { 'ax-host-tools': hostMcpServer },
          settingSources: [],
          systemPrompt: { type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code' },
          continue: history.length > 0,
        },
      });

      for await (const msg of queryIter) {
        if (msg.type === 'assistant') {
          // Text blocks accumulate into an assistant ChatMessage for history.
          const text = msg.message.content
            .filter((b) => b.type === 'text')
            .map((b) => (b as { text: string }).text)
            .join('\n');
          if (text) history.push({ role: 'assistant', content: text });
        } else if (msg.type === 'result') {
          // Terminal. I7: turn-end fires here, not per-tool.
          await client
            .event('event.turn-end', { reason: 'user-message-wait' })
            .catch(() => {});
          break;
        }
      }
    }

    await client
      .event('event.chat-end', {
        outcome: { kind: 'complete', messages: history },
      })
      .catch(() => {});
    await client.close();
    return 0;
  } catch (err) {
    await client
      .event('event.chat-end', {
        outcome: {
          kind: 'terminated',
          reason: err instanceof Error ? err.name : 'unknown',
          error: err,
        },
      })
      .catch(() => {});
    await client.close();
    return 1;
  }
}
```

**Step 1: Run `security-checklist` skill for the proxy.**

Three threat models:

1. **Sandbox escape:** the proxy is HOST-SIDE, listening on `127.0.0.1`. Sandbox subprocess reaches it via the loopback interface. Risks: if the proxy's auth is bypassed, ANY local process with `127.0.0.1` reach could ask the host to issue LLM calls on behalf of a session. Mitigation: session bearer token check (`session:resolve-token`) before body read; per-session ephemeral port; body-size cap. Document in SECURITY.md.
2. **Prompt injection:** the proxy translates untrusted model output back-and-forth (tool results → user messages → LLM → assistant → proxy → claude-sdk). The `llm:pre-call` subscribers on the host side (canary, etc., when they arrive) intercept. The proxy does NOT need its own scrubbing — it's a translator, not a firewall. Document explicitly to prevent a future reviewer from adding scrubbing in the wrong layer.
3. **Supply chain:** `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` is a new top-level dep. Pin to an exact patch version. Document how to update (pnpm add with exact version, run acceptance test, security-checklist re-run).

**Step 2: Run `security-checklist` skill for the claude-sdk dependency tree.**

- The SDK bundles the claude-code CLI (a subprocess binary); its own deps land transitively. Spot-check the pnpm-lock diff for unexpected additions.
- Built-in tools that remain enabled (`Bash`, `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `TodoWrite`) all operate inside the sandbox subprocess tree. Verify each runs with cwd = workspaceRoot; `Bash` specifically inherits the sandbox's filesystem scope.
- `WebFetch`, `WebSearch` explicitly disabled via `disallowedTools` — document that this is a capability minimization, NOT merely a preference. A reviewer tempted to re-enable must go through security-checklist first.
- `Task` disabled: subagent-spawning is deferred until we have a story for IPC-level subagent sessions. Per `project_mvp_direction.md` this comes back Week 9.5+.
- `Skill` disabled: skills are our own conventions (see `ax-conventions`), not claude-sdk's. Enabling the SDK's skill loader would load user-level CLAUDE.md/skill dirs and break `settingSources: []`.

```

toolUseResult.content:
# Week 6.5d — claude-sdk runner Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Ship `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` (sandbox-side) + `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` (host-side) so AX can swap the sandbox runner to `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` via config, with claude-sdk's built-in tools AND host-mediated tools both firing the existing `tool:pre-call` / `tool:post-call` subscribers through the unchanged IPC contract.

**Architecture:**

- The runner wraps `query()` from `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. Built-in tools (`Bash`, `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `TodoWrite`) stay enabled and run inside the sandbox process tree (claude-sdk spawns subprocess descendants); `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `Skill`, `Task` are disabled via `disallowedTools`. Any `executesIn: 'host'` tool from `tool.list` is surfaced to the SDK through an in-process `createSdkMcpServer` whose handlers forward to `tool.execute-host` IPC.
- `canUseTool` callback issues `tool.pre-call` IPC (veto + rewrite); `hooks.PostToolUse` issues `event.tool-post-call` IPC (observation-only per D4). Works uniformly for built-ins and SDK-MCP tools.
- LLM traffic routes via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` → host-side `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` → `llm:call` service hook. The proxy translates Anthropic Messages API wire format (`POST /v1/messages` with multi-block content, `tool_use`/`tool_result`, system string, tools array, streaming SSE) into `@ax/core`'s flat `LlmRequest` shape and back. Session bearer token is reused as `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`; the proxy verifies it via `session:resolve-token`.
- No cross-plugin imports (I2). Schema lives in `@ax/ipc-protocol` (unchanged — 6.5d adds **no** new IPC actions; host-mediated tools reuse `tool.execute-host`).

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, pnpm workspace, Zod schemas, Node `http` (proxy listener), `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` (sandbox-side), vitest, existing `@ax/agent-runner-core` + `@ax/ipc-protocol` unchanged.

**Branch:** `feat/week-6.5d-claude-sdk-runner` off `main` (tip of 6.5a, which is `f07a6cf`). The kickoff prompt said "branch off 6.5c," but 6.5b/6.5c are skipped for MVP per `project_mvp_direction.md`. Flag this to reviewer in the PR description.

---

## Prior-slice deviations folded into invariants

6.5a (PR #6) surfaced the following lessons; 6.5d carries them forward as numbered invariants the reviewer can audit against.

- **I1 — runner holds no LLM credentials.** The sandbox never sees `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in the real sense. The env var passed to claude-sdk is the per-session bearer token (same token that authenticates IPC). The proxy verifies it via `session:resolve-token`, same path `@ax/ipc-server` uses.
- **I2 — schema source of truth stays in `@ax/ipc-protocol`.** 6.5d adds NO new IPC actions. `tool.execute-host` (already defined, schema-only in 6.5a) earns its runtime wiring here. If a field needs adding, add it in `@ax/ipc-protocol` in one task and consume it in the next.
- **I3 — proxy HTTP surface is a new attack surface.** Bind to `127.0.0.1` only, emit `Authorization: Bearer` check BEFORE any body parsing, enforce body-size cap identical to `@ax/ipc-server` (4 MiB), redact token in logs. Security-checklist below is mandatory.
- **I4 — no half-wired plugins.** Both packages are wired through to the acceptance test in the same PR. The runner binary is resolvable from `@ax/cli` via `createRequire` (same mechanism as `@ax/agent-native-runner`).
- **I5 — honest tool name passthrough.** The claude-sdk tool name (`Bash`, `Read`, …) is what flows to `tool:pre-call` subscribers. Do NOT remap to `bash` / `read_file` — that would forge the audit trail, and pi-session runner (Week 13+) would need its own remap. Subscribers that care about a specific tool handle the naming difference themselves.
- **I6 — no new dependency on `@ax/tool-bash-impl` / `@ax/tool-file-io-impl`.** claude-sdk ships its own Bash/Read/Write/Edit. The sandbox-side impl packages stay wired to `@ax/agent-native-runner` only; the claude-sdk runner does not import them.
- **I7 — turn boundary = `SDKResultMessage`.** `event.turn-end` and `event.chat-end` fire on the SDK's terminal result, never mid-stream. Workspace commits (when Week 7–9 ships them) happen here; do not emit them per-tool.
- **I8 — streaming SSE from the proxy is synthesized, not pass-through.** IPC `llm.call` is non-streaming. The proxy issues one IPC call per `/v1/messages` request and then emits faux SSE frames (`message_start`, `content_block_start`, `content_block_delta`, `content_block_stop`, `message_delta`, `message_stop`) to satisfy claude-code CLI's streaming expectations. No partial-token fan-out in 6.5d; 6.5b-style streaming is Week 13+.
- **I9 — proxy listener lifecycle mirrors IPC listener.** Per-session bind (ephemeral port on 127.0.0.1), started alongside `ipc:start` and torn down alongside `ipc:stop`. Host-wide singleton is a k8s concern (Week 7–9), not MVP.

---

## Scope decisions this plan locks in

Surfaced by the design doc for 6.5d; each needs an explicit call before code lands.

1. **Streaming vs non-streaming proxy.** Support BOTH. claude-code CLI defaults to `stream: true`; if we ship non-streaming only we lose the built-in. Non-streaming path: direct bulk JSON response translated from one `llm:call`. Streaming path: one `llm:call` IPC, synthesize SSE frames from the bulk response. Token-level streaming from the provider is NOT re-streamed (that's 6.5b work we skipped).

2. **Tool name convention.** Pass the claude-sdk tool name verbatim to `tool:pre-call` and `tool:post-call`. MCP tools reaching claude-sdk via `createSdkMcpServer` carry the `mcp__ax-host-tools__<toolname>` prefix the SDK synthesizes; our `tool:pre-call` subscriber receives this prefixed name. The `executeToolHost` IPC dispatch inside the runner strips the prefix before calling `tool.execute-host` (host expects the bare tool name).

3. **MCP server grouping.** One `createSdkMcpServer({ name: 'ax-host-tools', tools: [...] })` per runner. All `executesIn: 'host'` tools from `tool.list` register into this one server. Keeps the tool namespace flat and the SDK surface predictable.

4. **Disabled built-ins.** `disallowedTools: ['WebFetch', 'WebSearch', 'Skill', 'Task']`. Do NOT use `allowedTools` (whitelist) — that would require re-enumerating every SDK-MCP tool and is fragile when the SDK adds new built-ins.

5. **Settings sources.** `settingSources: []` (claude-sdk-docs default). We do NOT want CLAUDE.md / project settings bleeding into the sandbox session. AX owns the context.

6. **System prompt.** `systemPrompt: { type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code' }`. The runner's job is to be claude-sdk; reinventing the system prompt here would defeat the point. Future: allow CLI config to append.

7. **`pathToClaudeCodeExecutable`.** Leave unset. The SDK bundles its own claude-code CLI. Documented side effect: the sandbox `node_modules` must contain `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` at runtime — covered by the runner package's dependency and pnpm's hoisting.

8. **Proxy auth scheme.** Reuse the existing session bearer token. No new auth boundary. The proxy calls `session:resolve-token` to validate — same check `@ax/ipc-server` does.

9. **Proxy transport.** Plain HTTP on `127.0.0.1:<ephemeral>` per session, URL injected via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:<port>`. The sandbox subprocess inherits from the host's network namespace so this works. k8s / Docker sandbox plugins will have to reintroduce routing (Week 7–9); the plan documents this as a known extension point.

10. **Built-in vs MCP `tool:pre-call` parity.** Per design doc D4 + the acceptance test: every tool call — built-in or MCP — must fire `tool:pre-call` with a `ToolCall` that has `{id, name, input}` and respect the verdict. `canUseTool` gives us `toolName` + `input` only (no tool_use_id), so we synthesize a runner-local uuid for the IPC call. PostToolUse gets the real tool_use_id; we emit that as the `id` on `event.tool-post-call`. Subscribers correlating pre↔post by id should not (per D4, post is observation-only); we do not expose a correlation contract in 6.5d.

---

## Bite-sized tasks

Each task ends with a test + a commit. Frequent commits. DRY. YAGNI.

### Task 1: Branch + package scaffolding

**Files:**
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/package.json`
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/tsconfig.json`
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/index.ts`
- Create: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/package.json`
- Create: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/tsconfig.json`
- Create: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts`
- Modify: `pnpm-workspace.yaml` (no change if using `packages/*` glob — confirm)
- Modify: `tsconfig.json` (refs)

```bash
git checkout main && git pull && git checkout -b feat/week-6.5d-claude-sdk-runner
```

**Step 2: Scaffold `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` package.json.**

Match `@ax/ipc-server`'s shape (ESM, tsc build, vitest test). Dependencies: `@ax/core` (workspace), `@ax/ipc-protocol` (workspace), `zod`. No runtime dependency on `@ax/session-inmemory` — coordinate via hooks (I2).

**Step 3: Scaffold `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` package.json.**

Match `@ax/agent-native-runner`'s shape (binary via `bin` field, tsc build). Dependencies: `@ax/agent-runner-core` (workspace), `@ax/ipc-protocol` (workspace), `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` (latest), `zod`. NO dep on `@ax/tool-bash-impl` / `@ax/tool-file-io-impl` (I6).

**Step 4: Write placeholder `src/index.ts` and `src/main.ts` that compile and `process.exit(2)` respectively.**

**Step 5: Update workspace TS refs.**

Add both packages to `tsconfig.json`'s `references`. `pnpm install` from repo root. `pnpm build` compiles with empty packages.

**Step 6: Commit.**

```bash
git add packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner tsconfig.json
git commit -m "scaffold(6.5d): @ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format + @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner skeletons"
```

---

### Task 2: Anthropic-format ↔ LlmRequest translation (proxy, pure functions)

Do this FIRST, before any HTTP. Pure functions are test-driven cleanly.

### Task 10: Wire `query()` — runner main entry point

**Files:**
- Modify: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts`

**Step 1: Write main body.**

```ts
import { query } from '@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk';
import {
  createIpcClient,
  createInboxLoop,
} from '@ax/agent-runner-core';
import type {
  ChatMessage,
  ToolListResponse,
  ToolDescriptor,
} from '@ax/ipc-protocol';
import { readRunnerEnv } from './env.js';
import { createCanUseTool } from './can-use-tool.js';
import { createPostToolUseHook } from './post-tool-use.js';
import { createHostMcpServer } from './host-mcp-server.js';

const DISABLED_BUILTINS = ['WebFetch', 'WebSearch', 'Skill', 'Task'] as const;

export async function main(): Promise<number> {
  const env = readRunnerEnv();
  const client = createIpcClient({
    socketPath: env.ipcSocket,
    token: env.authToken,
  });

  const { tools } = (await client.call('tool.list', {})) as ToolListResponse;
  const hostMcpServer = createHostMcpServer({ client, tools });

  const inbox = createInboxLoop({ client });
  const history: ChatMessage[] = [];

  try {
    for (;;) {
      const entry = await inbox.next();
      if (entry.type === 'cancel') break;
      if (entry.payload === undefined) continue;

      const userText = entry.payload.content;

      // claude-sdk's `query()` accepts string OR async iterable. We use a
      // string per call since AX's inbox model is one-user-message-per-turn.
      // `continue: true` on subsequent calls reuses the prior session.
      const queryIter = query({
        prompt: userText,
        options: {
          env: {
            ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL: env.llmProxyUrl,
            ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: env.authToken,
          },
          cwd: env.workspaceRoot,
          disallowedTools: [...DISABLED_BUILTINS],
          canUseTool: createCanUseTool({ client }),
          hooks: {
            PostToolUse: [
              { hooks: [createPostToolUseHook({ client })] },
            ],
          },
          mcpServers: { 'ax-host-tools': hostMcpServer },
          settingSources: [],
          systemPrompt: { type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code' },
          continue: history.length > 0,
        },
      });

      for await (const msg of queryIter) {
        if (msg.type === 'assistant') {
          // Text blocks accumulate into an assistant ChatMessage for history.
          const text = msg.message.content
            .filter((b) => b.type === 'text')
            .map((b) => (b as { text: string }).text)
            .join('\n');
          if (text) history.push({ role: 'assistant', content: text });
        } else if (msg.type === 'result') {
          // Terminal. I7: turn-end fires here, not per-tool.
          await client
            .event('event.turn-end', { reason: 'user-message-wait' })
            .catch(() => {});
          break;
        }
      }
    }

    await client
      .event('event.chat-end', {
        outcome: { kind: 'complete', messages: history },
      })
      .catch(() => {});
    await client.close();
    return 0;
  } catch (err) {
    await client
      .event('event.chat-end', {
        outcome: {
          kind: 'terminated',
          reason: err instanceof Error ? err.name : 'unknown',
          error: err,
        },
      })
      .catch(() => {});
    await client.close();
    return 1;
  }
}
```

### Task 15: Security checklist — `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` + claude-sdk deps

**REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Invoke `security-checklist` skill twice — once for the proxy HTTP surface, once for the `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` dependency tree.**

**Files:**
- Create: `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/SECURITY.md`
- Create: `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/SECURITY.md`
- Modify: PR description per the skill's output.

**Step 1: Run `security-checklist` skill for the proxy.**

Three threat models:

1. **Sandbox escape:** the proxy is HOST-SIDE, listening on `127.0.0.1`. Sandbox subprocess reaches it via the loopback interface. Risks: if the proxy's auth is bypassed, ANY local process with `127.0.0.1` reach could ask the host to issue LLM calls on behalf of a session. Mitigation: session bearer token check (`session:resolve-token`) before body read; per-session ephemeral port; body-size cap. Document in SECURITY.md.
2. **Prompt injection:** the proxy translates untrusted model output back-and-forth (tool results → user messages → LLM → assistant → proxy → claude-sdk). The `llm:pre-call` subscribers on the host side (canary, etc., when they arrive) intercept. The proxy does NOT need its own scrubbing — it's a translator, not a firewall. Document explicitly to prevent a future reviewer from adding scrubbing in the wrong layer.
3. **Supply chain:** `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` is a new top-level dep. Pin to an exact patch version. Document how to update (pnpm add with exact version, run acceptance test, security-checklist re-run).

**Step 2: Run `security-checklist` skill for the claude-sdk dependency tree.**

- The SDK bundles the claude-code CLI (a subprocess binary); its own deps land transitively. Spot-check the pnpm-lock diff for unexpected additions.
- Built-in tools that remain enabled (`Bash`, `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `TodoWrite`) all operate inside the sandbox subprocess tree. Verify each runs with cwd = workspaceRoot; `Bash` specifically inherits the sandbox's filesystem scope.
- `WebFetch`, `WebSearch` explicitly disabled via `disallowedTools` — document that this is a capability minimization, NOT merely a preference. A reviewer tempted to re-enable must go through security-checklist first.
- `Task` disabled: subagent-spawning is deferred until we have a story for IPC-level subagent sessions. Per `project_mvp_direction.md` this comes back Week 9.5+.
- `Skill` disabled: skills are our own conventions (see `ax-conventions`), not claude-sdk's. Enabling the SDK's skill loader would load user-level CLAUDE.md/skill dirs and break `settingSources: []`.

**Step 3: Commit both SECURITY.md files.**

```bash
git commit -am "docs(6.5d): SECURITY.md for llm-proxy + claude-sdk-runner (security-checklist output)"
```

---

### Task 16: Changeset + PR notes

**Files:**
- Create: `.changeset/week-6-5d-claude-sdk-runner.md`
- Create: `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5d-pr-notes.md`

**Step 1: Changeset.**

Mark `@ax/cli`, `@ax/sandbox-subprocess`, `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`, `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format`, `@ax/test-harness` as minor. Every other package patch-only.

**Step 2: PR notes — brief, reviewer-focused.**

Cover: scope (two new packages), invariants I1–I9 and where they're enforced, scope decisions 1–10 and their rationale, security-checklist summary, acceptance test location, **open issues** (CodeRabbit / boundary-review items) to call out proactively. Structure lifted from `2026-04-24-week-6.5a-pr-notes.md`.

**Step 3: Build + full test.**

```bash
pnpm build && pnpm test
```
Expected: PASS across all packages.

**Step 4: Commit + push.**

```bash
git add .changeset docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5d-pr-notes.md
git commit -m "chore(6.5d): changeset + PR notes"
git push -u origin feat/week-6.5d-claude-sdk-runner
```

**Step 5: Open PR.**

In the PR description, explicitly flag:
- Branch target is `main` (not 6.5c) because 6.5b/6.5c were skipped for MVP.
- Two new packages, both fully wired (I4, no half-wired plugins).
- Security-checklist output per `SECURITY.md` files.
- Cross-reference `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5d-claude-sdk-runner.md` and `2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md`.

---

## Acceptance summary

- `pnpm build && pnpm test` clean.
- `ax-next` with `ax.config.ts` setting `runner: 'claude-sdk'` launches claude-sdk runner subprocess; built-in `Bash` and stub MCP host tool both fire `tool:pre-call` and `tool:post-call` subscribers through the existing IPC contract; final assistant message returns to CLI stdout; rc = 0.
- Security-checklist output committed for both new packages.

---

## Out of scope

- Token-level streaming (6.5b-style incremental rendering) — still deferred.
- Real MCP servers (user-configured stdio / http / sse) — that's 6.5e per `2026-04-24-week-6.5e-mcp-client-handoff.md`.
- Native-runner feature parity across all claude-sdk built-ins — native runner retains its existing `bash` / `read_file` / `write_file`; the two runners are parallel, not interchangeable behind every workflow.
- k8s sandbox & multi-replica proxy topology — Week 7–9.
- Image content-block support in the proxy — Week 13+.
- `llm:pre-call` / `llm:post-call` subscriber hooks wired for transformation — scope of a future slice when the first subscriber (canary) earns its keep.

content.text:
Plan complete and saved to `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5d-claude-sdk-runner.md`.

**One plan-vs-reality deviation flagged in the plan header:** the prompt said "branch off 6.5c," but 6.5b and 6.5c were skipped for MVP per `project_mvp_direction.md` — so the branch target is `main` (tip of 6.5a, `f07a6cf`). The plan states this explicitly and surfaces it in the PR notes (Task 16).

**Scope summary:**
- **`@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format`** (host): `POST /v1/messages` HTTP listener on `127.0.0.1:<ephemeral>`, translates Anthropic wire format ↔ `LlmRequest`, synthesizes SSE frames, session-bearer-token auth via `session:resolve-token`.
- **`@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`** (sandbox): wraps `query()` with `canUseTool` → `tool.pre-call` IPC, `hooks.PostToolUse` → `event.tool-post-call` IPC, `createSdkMcpServer('ax-host-tools')` for `executesIn: 'host'` tools, `disallowedTools: ['WebFetch','WebSearch','Skill','Task']`.
- **No new IPC actions** — `tool.execute-host` (already schema-defined in 6.5a) earns its runtime wiring here.
- **16 tasks**, TDD-first (pure translators before HTTP, adapters before wiring), full `pnpm build && pnpm test` green at each commit.
- **Security-checklist invoked twice** (Task 15) — proxy HTTP surface + claude-sdk dependency tree — with `SECURITY.md` output for each package.

Acceptance test (Task 14) uses a stub `test-host-echo` (added to `@ax/test-harness` in Task 13) to exercise both the built-in-tool and MCP-host-tool paths in one run.

**Two execution options:**

1. **Subagent-Driven (this session)** — I dispatch a fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration.
2. **Parallel Session (separate)** — open a new session (ideally in a worktree) with `superpowers:executing-plans`, batch execution with checkpoints.

Which approach?
